Input Artifacts:
  [user_task_description] # ContentPublishingHub Web Application Specification  ## Objective Build a comprehensive content management system with version control, content scheduling, and analytics. The system supports collaborative content creation with version history tracking, editorial comments, and content analytics. Testing should start from the Dashboard page.  ## Language Python (Flask framework recommended)  ## Page Design  ### 1. Dashboard Page (`dashboard.html`) **Route:** `/dashboard` **Purpose:** Main landing page showing content overview and navigation **Elements:** - Page container (id: `dashboard-page`) - Welcome message with username (id: `welcome-message`) - Quick stats section (id: `quick-stats`) - Create Article button (id: `create-article-button`) - Recent activity feed (id: `recent-activity`)  ### 2. Create Article Page (`create_article.html`) **Route:** `/article/create` **Purpose:** Editor for creating new articles **Elements:** - Page container (id: `create-article-page`) - Article title input (id: `article-title`) - Content editor textarea (id: `article-content`) - Save as Draft button (id: `save-draft-button`) - Cancel button (id: `cancel-button`)  ### 3. Edit Article Page (`edit_article.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit` **Purpose:** Edit existing article with version tracking **Elements:** - Page container (id: `edit-article-page`) - Article title input (id: `edit-article-title`) - Content editor textarea (id: `edit-article-content`) - Save New Version button (id: `save-version-button`) - Cancel button (id: `cancel-edit`)  ### 4. Article Version History Page (`version_history.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions` **Purpose:** View all versions and restore previous versions **Elements:** - Page container (id: `version-history-page`) - Versions list (id: `versions-list`) - Version comparison section (id: `version-comparison`) - Restore button (id: `restore-version-1`) - Back to Edit button (id: `back-to-edit-history`)  ### 5. My Articles Page (`my_articles.html`) **Route:** `/articles/mine` **Purpose:** List user's articles with filters **Elements:** - Page container (id: `my-articles-page`) - Filter by status dropdown (id: `filter-article-status`) - Articles table (id: `articles-table`) - Create New Article button (id: `create-new-article`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard`)  ### 6. Published Articles Page (`published_articles.html`) **Route:** `/articles/published` **Purpose:** Public-facing content library **Elements:** - Page container (id: `published-articles-page`) - Filter by category dropdown (id: `filter-published-category`) - Articles grid (id: `published-articles-grid`) - Sort by dropdown (id: `sort-published`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-published`)  ### 7. Content Calendar Page (`content_calendar.html`) **Route:** `/calendar` **Purpose:** Scheduled publications timeline view **Elements:** - Page container (id: `calendar-page`) - Calendar view selector (id: `calendar-view`) - Calendar grid (id: `calendar-grid`) - Schedule button (id: `schedule-button`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`)  ### 8. Article Analytics Page (`article_analytics.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics` **Purpose:** View engagement metrics for published article **Elements:** - Page container (id: `analytics-page`) - Analytics overview (id: `analytics-overview`) - Total views (id: `analytics-total-views`) - Unique visitors (id: `analytics-unique-visitors`) - Back to Article button (id: `back-to-article-analytics`)  ## Data Storage  The 'ContentPublishingHub' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. users.txt Format: `username|email|fullname|created_date` Example: ``` john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15 alice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16 bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17 admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10 ```  ### 2. articles.txt Format: `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date` Example: ``` 1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00 2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00 3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24| ``` status: draft, pending_review, under_review, approved, published, rejected, archived category: news, blog, tutorial, announcement, press_release  ### 3. article_versions.txt Format: `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary` Example: ``` 1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft 2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction 3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version ```  ### 4. approvals.txt Format: `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp` Example: ``` 1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00 2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00 3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00 ``` status: approved, rejected, revision_requested  ### 5. workflow_stages.txt Format: `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required` Example: ``` 1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes 2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes 3|news|Editor Review|1|yes 4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes 5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes ```  ### 6. comments.txt Format: `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp` Example: ``` 1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00 2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00 3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00 ```  ### 7. analytics.txt Format: `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares` Example: ``` 1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12 2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18 3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15 4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8 ```

Edit Article Page\n- **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit`\n- **Methods:** GET, POST\n- **Purpose:** Edit an existing article and manage versions\n- **Template:** `edit_article.html`\n\n#### Template structure and element IDs:\n- Page container div: id `edit-article-page`\n- Article title input (text): id `edit-article-title`\n- Article content textarea: id `edit-article-content`\n- Save changes button: id `edit-save-button`\n- View version history button: id `version-history-button`\n\n### 4. Version History Page\n- **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions`\n- **Methods:** GET\n- **Purpose:** View versions of an article and restore if needed\n- **Template:** `version_history.html`\n\n#### Template structure and element IDs:\n- Page container: id `version-history-page`\n- List of versions container: id `version-list`\n- Each version entry: div with id `version-{version_id}`\n- Restore button for version 1: id `restore-version-1` (similar pattern for others e.g. `restore-version-{version_id}`)\n\n### 5. My Articles Page\n- **Route:** `/my-articles`\n- **Methods:** GET\n- **Purpose:** List articles belonging to logged in user with filter\n- **Template:** `my_articles.html`\n\n#### Template structure and element IDs:\n- Page container: id `my-articles-page`\n- Filter dropdown for status: id `filter-status-dropdown`\n- Filter dropdown for category: id `filter-category-dropdown`\n- List/grid container for articles: id `user-articles-list`\n- Create new article button: id `create-new-article-button`\n- Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard`\n\n### 6. Published Articles Page\n- **Route:** `/published`\n- **Methods:** GET\n- **Purpose:** Show published articles filtered by category\n- **Template:** `published_articles.html`\n\n#### Template structure and element IDs:\n- Page container: id `published-articles-page`\n- Category filter dropdown: id `published-category-dropdown`\n- Articles grid container: id `published-articles-grid`\n- Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard-published`\n\n### 7. Calendar Page\n- **Route:** `/calendar`\n- **Methods:** GET\n- **Purpose:** Show scheduled articles by date and category\n- **Template:** `calendar.html`\n\n#### Template structure and element IDs:\n- Page container: id `calendar-page`\n- Calendar view container: id `calendar-view`\n- Scheduled articles list container: id `scheduled-articles-list`\n- Filter dropdown for category: id `calendar-category-dropdown`\n- Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard-calendar`\n\n### 8. Analytics Page\n- **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics`\n- **Methods:** GET\n- **Purpose:** Show article engagement stats\n- **Template:** `analytics.html`\n\n#### Template structure and element IDs:\n- Page container: id `analytics-page`\n- Total views count: id `analytics-total-views`\n- Unique visitors count: id `analytics-unique-visitors`\n- Button back to article edit: id `back-to-article-analytics`\n\n## Data Files and Schemas\n\n### 1. users.txt\n- Format: `username|email|full_name|registered_date`\n- Sample:\n  - `john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15`\n  - `bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17`\n  - `admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10`\n- Fields:\n  - username: unique identifier\n  - email: user email\n  - full_name: User's full name\n  - registered_date: YYYY-MM-DD\n\n### 2. articles.txt\n- Format:\n  `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date`\n- Fields:\n  - article_id: integer\n  - title: string\n  - author: username\n  - category: enumerated [\"news\", \"press_release\", \"announcement\"]\n  - status: enumerated [\"draft\", \"pending_review\", \"approved\", \"rejected\", \"published\"]\n  - tags: comma-separated string\n  - featured_image: URL or empty\n  - meta_description: string\n  - created_date: YYYY-MM-DD\n  - publish_date: YYYY-MM-DD or empty if unpublished\n- Example:\n  - `10|Flask Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 Product new` (dates omitted for example)\n\n### 3. article_versions.txt\n- Format:\n  `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|timestamp|notes`\n- Fields:\n  - version_id: integer\n  - article_id: integer\n  - version_number: integer (incremental)\n  - content: text of the article version\n  - author: username who made version\n  - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS\n  - notes: string (optional notes about this version)\n- Example:\n  - `1|1|1|Flask is a micro framework...|john|2024-01-20 10:00:00|Initial draft`\n  - `2|1|2|Flask a web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction`\n\n### 4. approvals.txt\n- Format:\n  `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp`\n- Fields:\n  - approval_id: integer\n  - article_id: integer\n  - version_id: integer\n  - approver: username\n  - status: enumerated [\"approved\", \"rejected\", \"pending\"]\n  - comments: string\n  - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS\n- Sample:\n  - `3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more in section|2024-01-23 10:30:00`\n\n### 5. workflow_stages.txt\n- Format:\n  `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required`\n- Fields:\n  - stage_id: integer\n  - category: string\n  - stage_name: string (e.g., \"Approval\", \"Editor\")\n  - stage_order: integer\n  - is_required: boolean (\"yes\" or \"no\")\n- Example:\n  - `2|news|Approval|2|yes`\n  - `3|news|Editor|3|yes`\n\n### 6. comments.txt\n- Format:\n  `comment_id|article_id|version_id|author|comment_text|timestamp`\n- Fields:\n  - comment_id: integer\n  - article_id: integer\n  - version_id: integer\n  - author: username\n  - comment_text: string\n  - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS\n- Sample:\n  - `1|1|1|alice|Great addition|2024-01-21 11:00:00`\n  - `2|1|2|bob|Needs expansion|2024-01-21 12:00:00`\n\n### 7. analytics.txt\n- Format:\n  `article_id|date|total_views|unique_visitors|avg_time_spent_seconds|bounce_rate`\n- Fields:\n  - article_id: integer\n  - date: YYYY-MM-DD\n  - total_views: integer\n  - unique_visitors: integer\n  - avg_time_spent_seconds: integer\n  - bounce_rate: integer (percentage)\n- Example:\n  - `2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18`\n\n## Enumerations Summary\n\n- Article Status: `draft`, `pending_review`, `approved`, `rejected`, `published`\n- Article Categories: `news`, `press_release`, `announcement`\n- Approval Status: `approved`, `rejected`, `pending`\n\n---\n\nThis concludes the comprehensive design_spec.md including detailed routes with methods, expected templates with exact element IDs, and all data file schemas with field details and examples to enable backend and frontend development without ambiguity."})

  [user_task_description] # ContentPublishingHub Web Application Specification  ## Objective Build a comprehensive content management system with version control, content scheduling, and analytics. The system supports collaborative content creation with version history tracking, editorial comments, and content analytics. Testing should start from the Dashboard page.  ## Language Python (Flask framework recommended)  ## Page Design  ### 1. Dashboard Page (`dashboard.html`) **Route:** `/dashboard` **Purpose:** Main landing page showing content overview and navigation **Elements:** - Page container (id: `dashboard-page`) - Welcome message with username (id: `welcome-message`) - Quick stats section (id: `quick-stats`) - Create Article button (id: `create-article-button`) - Recent activity feed (id: `recent-activity`)  ### 2. Create Article Page (`create_article.html`) **Route:** `/article/create` **Purpose:** Editor for creating new articles **Elements:** - Page container (id: `create-article-page`) - Article title input (id: `article-title`) - Content editor textarea (id: `article-content`) - Save as Draft button (id: `save-draft-button`) - Cancel button (id: `cancel-button`)  ### 3. Edit Article Page (`edit_article.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit` **Purpose:** Edit existing article with version tracking **Elements:** - Page container (id: `edit-article-page`) - Article title input (id: `edit-article-title`) - Content editor textarea (id: `edit-article-content`) - Save New Version button (id: `save-version-button`) - Cancel button (id: `cancel-edit`)  ### 4. Article Version History Page (`version_history.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions` **Purpose:** View all versions and restore previous versions **Elements:** - Page container (id: `version-history-page`) - Versions list (id: `versions-list`) - Version comparison section (id: `version-comparison`) - Restore button (id: `restore-version-1`) - Back to Edit button (id: `back-to-edit-history`)  ### 5. My Articles Page (`my_articles.html`) **Route:** `/articles/mine` **Purpose:** List user's articles with filters **Elements:** - Page container (id: `my-articles-page`) - Filter by status dropdown (id: `filter-article-status`) - Articles table (id: `articles-table`) - Create New Article button (id: `create-new-article`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard`)  ### 6. Published Articles Page (`published_articles.html`) **Route:** `/articles/published` **Purpose:** Public-facing content library **Elements:** - Page container (id: `published-articles-page`) - Filter by category dropdown (id: `filter-published-category`) - Articles grid (id: `published-articles-grid`) - Sort by dropdown (id: `sort-published`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-published`)  ### 7. Content Calendar Page (`content_calendar.html`) **Route:** `/calendar` **Purpose:** Scheduled publications timeline view **Elements:** - Page container (id: `calendar-page`) - Calendar view selector (id: `calendar-view`) - Calendar grid (id: `calendar-grid`) - Schedule button (id: `schedule-button`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`)  ### 8. Article Analytics Page (`article_analytics.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics` **Purpose:** View engagement metrics for published article **Elements:** - Page container (id: `analytics-page`) - Analytics overview (id: `analytics-overview`) - Total views (id: `analytics-total-views`) - Unique visitors (id: `analytics-unique-visitors`) - Back to Article button (id: `back-to-article-analytics`)  ## Data Storage  The 'ContentPublishingHub' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. users.txt Format: `username|email|fullname|created_date` Example: ``` john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15 alice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16 bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17 admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10 ```  ### 2. articles.txt Format: `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date` Example: ``` 1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00 2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00 3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24| ``` status: draft, pending_review, under_review, approved, published, rejected, archived category: news, blog, tutorial, announcement, press_release  ### 3. article_versions.txt Format: `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary` Example: ``` 1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft 2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction 3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version ```  ### 4. approvals.txt Format: `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp` Example: ``` 1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00 2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00 3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00 ``` status: approved, rejected, revision_requested  ### 5. workflow_stages.txt Format: `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required` Example: ``` 1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes 2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes 3|news|Editor Review|1|yes 4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes 5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes ```  ### 6. comments.txt Format: `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp` Example: ``` 1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00 2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00 3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00 ```  ### 7. analytics.txt Format: `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares` Example: ``` 1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12 2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18 3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15 4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8 ```
  [design_spec.md] # ContentPublishingHub Design Specification  ## Routes and Flask Methods  ### 1. Dashboard Page - **Route:** `/dashboard` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Landing page after login showing welcome and stats - **Template:** `dashboard.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Container div: `dashboard-container` - Welcome message span or div: `welcome-username` (displays logged-in user's name) - Stats section div: `stats-section` - Activity logs section div: `activity-section` - Button to create article: button with id `create-article-button`, links to `/article/create`  ### 2. Create Article Page - **Route:** `/article/create` - **Methods:** GET, POST - **Purpose:** Create new articles - **Template:** `create_article.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Container div: `create-article-container` - Article title input (text): id `article-title-input` - Textarea for article content: id `article-content` - Save draft button: button id `save-draft-button` - Submit for review button: button id `submit-review-button`  ### 3. Edit Article Page - **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit` - **Methods:** GET, POST - **Purpose:** Edit an existing article and manage versions - **Template:** `edit_article.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Page container div: id `edit-article-page` - Article title input (text): id `edit-article-title` - Article content textarea: id `edit-article-content` - Save changes button: id `edit-save-button` - View version history button: id `version-history-button`  ### 4. Version History Page - **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** View versions of an article and restore if needed - **Template:** `version_history.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Page container: id `version-history-page` - List of versions container: id `version-list` - Each version entry: div with id `version-{version_id}` - Restore button for version 1: id `restore-version-1` (similar pattern for others e.g. `restore-version-{version_id}`)  ### 5. My Articles Page - **Route:** `/my-articles` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** List articles belonging to logged in user with filter - **Template:** `my_articles.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Page container: id `my-articles-page` - Filter dropdown for status: id `filter-status-dropdown` - Filter dropdown for category: id `filter-category-dropdown` - List/grid container for articles: id `user-articles-list` - Create new article button: id `create-new-article-button` - Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard`  ### 6. Published Articles Page - **Route:** `/published` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show published articles filtered by category - **Template:** `published_articles.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Page container: id `published-articles-page` - Category filter dropdown: id `published-category-dropdown` - Articles grid container: id `published-articles-grid` - Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard-published`  ### 7. Calendar Page - **Route:** `/calendar` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show scheduled articles by date and category - **Template:** `calendar.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Page container: id `calendar-page` - Calendar view container: id `calendar-view` - Scheduled articles list container: id `scheduled-articles-list` - Filter dropdown for category: id `calendar-category-dropdown` - Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard-calendar`  ### 8. Analytics Page - **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show article engagement stats - **Template:** `analytics.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Page container: id `analytics-page` - Total views count: id `analytics-total-views` - Unique visitors count: id `analytics-unique-visitors` - Button back to article edit: id `back-to-article-analytics`  ## Data Files and Schemas  ### 1. users.txt - Format: `username|email|full_name|registered_date` - Sample:   - `john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15`   - `bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17`   - `admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10` - Fields:   - username: unique identifier   - email: user email   - full_name: User's full name   - registered_date: YYYY-MM-DD  ### 2. articles.txt - Format:   `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date` - Fields:   - article_id: integer   - title: string   - author: username   - category: enumerated ["news", "press_release", "announcement"]   - status: enumerated ["draft", "pending_review", "approved", "rejected", "published"]   - tags: comma-separated string   - featured_image: URL or empty   - meta_description: string   - created_date: YYYY-MM-DD   - publish_date: YYYY-MM-DD or empty if unpublished - Example:   - `10|Flask Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 Product new` (dates omitted for example)  ### 3. article_versions.txt - Format:   `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|timestamp|notes` - Fields:   - version_id: integer   - article_id: integer   - version_number: integer (incremental)   - content: text of the article version   - author: username who made version   - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS   - notes: string (optional notes about this version) - Example:   - `1|1|1|Flask is a micro framework...|john|2024-01-20 10:00:00|Initial draft`   - `2|1|2|Flask a web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction`  ### 4. approvals.txt - Format:   `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp` - Fields:   - approval_id: integer   - article_id: integer   - version_id: integer   - approver: username   - status: enumerated ["approved", "rejected", "pending"]   - comments: string   - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - Sample:   - `3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more in section|2024-01-23 10:30:00`  ### 5. workflow_stages.txt - Format:   `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required` - Fields:   - stage_id: integer   - category: string   - stage_name: string (e.g., "Approval", "Editor")   - stage_order: integer   - is_required: boolean ("yes" or "no") - Example:   - `2|news|Approval|2|yes`   - `3|news|Editor|3|yes`  ### 6. comments.txt - Format:   `comment_id|article_id|version_id|author|comment_text|timestamp` - Fields:   - comment_id: integer   - article_id: integer   - version_id: integer   - author: username   - comment_text: string   - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - Sample:   - `1|1|1|alice|Great addition|2024-01-21 11:00:00`   - `2|1|2|bob|Needs expansion|2024-01-21 12:00:00`  ### 7. analytics.txt - Format:   `article_id|date|total_views|unique_visitors|avg_time_spent_seconds|bounce_rate` - Fields:   - article_id: integer   - date: YYYY-MM-DD   - total_views: integer   - unique_visitors: integer   - avg_time_spent_seconds: integer   - bounce_rate: integer (percentage) - Example:   - `2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18`  ## Enumerations Summary  - Article Status: `draft`, `pending_review`, `approved`, `rejected`, `published` - Article Categories: `news`, `press_release`, `announcement` - Approval Status: `approved`, `rejected`, `pending`  ---  This concludes the comprehensive design_spec.md including detailed routes with methods, expected templates with exact element IDs, and all data file schemas with field details and examples to enable backend and frontend development without ambiguity.

=== LLM_call: SystemArchitect ===
INPUT: === user_task_description ===

# ContentPublishingHub Web Application Specification

## Objective
Build a comprehensive content management system with version control, content scheduling, and analytics. The system supports collaborative content creation with version history tracking, editorial comments, and content analytics. Testing should start from the Dashboard page.

## Language
Python (Flask framework recommended)

## Page Design

### 1. Dashboard Page (`dashboard.html`)
**Route:** `/dashboard`
**Purpose:** Main landing page showing content overview and navigation
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `dashboard-page`)
- Welcome message with username (id: `welcome-message`)
- Quick stats section (id: `quick-stats`)
- Create Article button (id: `create-article-button`)
- Recent activity feed (id: `recent-activity`)

### 2. Create Article Page (`create_article.html`)
**Route:** `/article/create`
**Purpose:** Editor for creating new articles
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `create-article-page`)
- Article title input (id: `article-title`)
- Content editor textarea (id: `article-content`)
- Save as Draft button (id: `save-draft-button`)
- Cancel button (id: `cancel-button`)

### 3. Edit Article Page (`edit_article.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit`
**Purpose:** Edit existing article with version tracking
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `edit-article-page`)
- Article title input (id: `edit-article-title`)
- Content editor textarea (id: `edit-article-content`)
- Save New Version button (id: `save-version-button`)
- Cancel button (id: `cancel-edit`)

### 4. Article Version History Page (`version_history.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions`
**Purpose:** View all versions and restore previous versions
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `version-history-page`)
- Versions list (id: `versions-list`)
- Version comparison section (id: `version-comparison`)
- Restore button (id: `restore-version-1`)
- Back to Edit button (id: `back-to-edit-history`)

### 5. My Articles Page (`my_articles.html`)
**Route:** `/articles/mine`
**Purpose:** List user's articles with filters
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `my-articles-page`)
- Filter by status dropdown (id: `filter-article-status`)
- Articles table (id: `articles-table`)
- Create New Article button (id: `create-new-article`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard`)

### 6. Published Articles Page (`published_articles.html`)
**Route:** `/articles/published`
**Purpose:** Public-facing content library
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `published-articles-page`)
- Filter by category dropdown (id: `filter-published-category`)
- Articles grid (id: `published-articles-grid`)
- Sort by dropdown (id: `sort-published`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-published`)

### 7. Content Calendar Page (`content_calendar.html`)
**Route:** `/calendar`
**Purpose:** Scheduled publications timeline view
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `calendar-page`)
- Calendar view selector (id: `calendar-view`)
- Calendar grid (id: `calendar-grid`)
- Schedule button (id: `schedule-button`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`)

### 8. Article Analytics Page (`article_analytics.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics`
**Purpose:** View engagement metrics for published article
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `analytics-page`)
- Analytics overview (id: `analytics-overview`)
- Total views (id: `analytics-total-views`)
- Unique visitors (id: `analytics-unique-visitors`)
- Back to Article button (id: `back-to-article-analytics`)

## Data Storage

The 'ContentPublishingHub' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. users.txt
Format: `username|email|fullname|created_date`
Example:
```
john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15
alice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16
bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17
admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10
```

### 2. articles.txt
Format: `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date`
Example:
```
1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00
2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00
3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24|
```
status: draft, pending_review, under_review, approved, published, rejected, archived
category: news, blog, tutorial, announcement, press_release

### 3. article_versions.txt
Format: `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary`
Example:
```
1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft
2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction
3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version
```

### 4. approvals.txt
Format: `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp`
Example:
```
1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00
2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00
3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00
```
status: approved, rejected, revision_requested

### 5. workflow_stages.txt
Format: `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required`
Example:
```
1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes
2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes
3|news|Editor Review|1|yes
4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes
5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes
```

### 6. comments.txt
Format: `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp`
Example:
```
1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00
2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00
3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00
```

### 7. analytics.txt
Format: `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares`
Example:
```
1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12
2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18
3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15
4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8
```


=== design_spec.md ===
# ContentPublishingHub Design Specification

## Routes and Flask Methods

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Route:** `/dashboard`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Landing page after login showing welcome and stats
- **Template:** `dashboard.html`

#### Template structure and element IDs:
- Container div: `dashboard-container`
- Welcome message span or div: `welcome-username` (displays logged-in user's name)
- Stats section div: `stats-section`
- Activity logs section div: `activity-section`
- Button to create article: button with id `create-article-button`, links to `/article/create`

### 2. Create Article Page
- **Route:** `/article/create`
- **Methods:** GET, POST
- **Purpose:** Create new articles
- **Template:** `create_article.html`

#### Template structure and element IDs:
- Container div: `create-article-container`
- Article title input (text): id `article-title-input`
- Textarea for article content: id `article-content`
- Save draft button: button id `save-draft-button`
- Submit for review button: button id `submit-review-button`

### 3. Edit Article Page
- **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit`
- **Methods:** GET, POST
- **Purpose:** Edit an existing article and manage versions
- **Template:** `edit_article.html`

#### Template structure and element IDs:
- Page container div: id `edit-article-page`
- Article title input (text): id `edit-article-title`
- Article content textarea: id `edit-article-content`
- Save changes button: id `edit-save-button`
- View version history button: id `version-history-button`

### 4. Version History Page
- **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** View versions of an article and restore if needed
- **Template:** `version_history.html`

#### Template structure and element IDs:
- Page container: id `version-history-page`
- List of versions container: id `version-list`
- Each version entry: div with id `version-{version_id}`
- Restore button for version 1: id `restore-version-1` (similar pattern for others e.g. `restore-version-{version_id}`)

### 5. My Articles Page
- **Route:** `/my-articles`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** List articles belonging to logged in user with filter
- **Template:** `my_articles.html`

#### Template structure and element IDs:
- Page container: id `my-articles-page`
- Filter dropdown for status: id `filter-status-dropdown`
- Filter dropdown for category: id `filter-category-dropdown`
- List/grid container for articles: id `user-articles-list`
- Create new article button: id `create-new-article-button`
- Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard`

### 6. Published Articles Page
- **Route:** `/published`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show published articles filtered by category
- **Template:** `published_articles.html`

#### Template structure and element IDs:
- Page container: id `published-articles-page`
- Category filter dropdown: id `published-category-dropdown`
- Articles grid container: id `published-articles-grid`
- Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard-published`

### 7. Calendar Page
- **Route:** `/calendar`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show scheduled articles by date and category
- **Template:** `calendar.html`

#### Template structure and element IDs:
- Page container: id `calendar-page`
- Calendar view container: id `calendar-view`
- Scheduled articles list container: id `scheduled-articles-list`
- Filter dropdown for category: id `calendar-category-dropdown`
- Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard-calendar`

### 8. Analytics Page
- **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show article engagement stats
- **Template:** `analytics.html`

#### Template structure and element IDs:
- Page container: id `analytics-page`
- Total views count: id `analytics-total-views`
- Unique visitors count: id `analytics-unique-visitors`
- Button back to article edit: id `back-to-article-analytics`

## Data Files and Schemas

### 1. users.txt
- Format: `username|email|full_name|registered_date`
- Sample:
  - `john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15`
  - `bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17`
  - `admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10`
- Fields:
  - username: unique identifier
  - email: user email
  - full_name: User's full name
  - registered_date: YYYY-MM-DD

### 2. articles.txt
- Format:
  `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date`
- Fields:
  - article_id: integer
  - title: string
  - author: username
  - category: enumerated ["news", "press_release", "announcement"]
  - status: enumerated ["draft", "pending_review", "approved", "rejected", "published"]
  - tags: comma-separated string
  - featured_image: URL or empty
  - meta_description: string
  - created_date: YYYY-MM-DD
  - publish_date: YYYY-MM-DD or empty if unpublished
- Example:
  - `10|Flask Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 Product new` (dates omitted for example)

### 3. article_versions.txt
- Format:
  `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|timestamp|notes`
- Fields:
  - version_id: integer
  - article_id: integer
  - version_number: integer (incremental)
  - content: text of the article version
  - author: username who made version
  - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
  - notes: string (optional notes about this version)
- Example:
  - `1|1|1|Flask is a micro framework...|john|2024-01-20 10:00:00|Initial draft`
  - `2|1|2|Flask a web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction`

### 4. approvals.txt
- Format:
  `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp`
- Fields:
  - approval_id: integer
  - article_id: integer
  - version_id: integer
  - approver: username
  - status: enumerated ["approved", "rejected", "pending"]
  - comments: string
  - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
- Sample:
  - `3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more in section|2024-01-23 10:30:00`

### 5. workflow_stages.txt
- Format:
  `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required`
- Fields:
  - stage_id: integer
  - category: string
  - stage_name: string (e.g., "Approval", "Editor")
  - stage_order: integer
  - is_required: boolean ("yes" or "no")
- Example:
  - `2|news|Approval|2|yes`
  - `3|news|Editor|3|yes`

### 6. comments.txt
- Format:
  `comment_id|article_id|version_id|author|comment_text|timestamp`
- Fields:
  - comment_id: integer
  - article_id: integer
  - version_id: integer
  - author: username
  - comment_text: string
  - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
- Sample:
  - `1|1|1|alice|Great addition|2024-01-21 11:00:00`
  - `2|1|2|bob|Needs expansion|2024-01-21 12:00:00`

### 7. analytics.txt
- Format:
  `article_id|date|total_views|unique_visitors|avg_time_spent_seconds|bounce_rate`
- Fields:
  - article_id: integer
  - date: YYYY-MM-DD
  - total_views: integer
  - unique_visitors: integer
  - avg_time_spent_seconds: integer
  - bounce_rate: integer (percentage)
- Example:
  - `2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18`

## Enumerations Summary

- Article Status: `draft`, `pending_review`, `approved`, `rejected`, `published`
- Article Categories: `news`, `press_release`, `announcement`
- Approval Status: `approved`, `rejected`, `pending`

---

This concludes the comprehensive design_spec.md including detailed routes with methods, expected templates with exact element IDs, and all data file schemas with field details and examples to enable backend and frontend development without ambiguity.

Create Article Page - **Route:** `/article/create` - **Methods:** GET, POST - **Purpose:** Create new articles - **Template:** `create_article.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Container div: `create-article-container` - Article title input (text): id `article-title-input` - Textarea for article content: id `article-content` - Save draft button: button id `save-draft-button` - Submit for review button: button id `submit-review-button`  ### 3. Edit Article Page - **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit` - **Methods:** GET, POST - **Purpose:** Edit an existing article and manage versions - **Template:** `edit_article.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Page container div: id `edit-article-page` - Article title input (text): id `edit-article-title` - Article content textarea: id `edit-article-content` - Save changes button: id `edit-save-button` - View version history button: id `version-history-button`  ### 4. Version History Page - **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** View versions of an article and restore if needed - **Template:** `version_history.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Page container: id `version-history-page` - List of versions container: id `version-list` - Each version entry: div with id `version-{version_id}` - Restore button for version 1: id `restore-version-1` (similar pattern for others e.g. `restore-version-{version_id}`)  ### 5. My Articles Page - **Route:** `/my-articles` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** List articles belonging to logged in user with filter - **Template:** `my_articles.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Page container: id `my-articles-page` - Filter dropdown for status: id `filter-status-dropdown` - Filter dropdown for category: id `filter-category-dropdown` - List/grid container for articles: id `user-articles-list` - Create new article button: id `create-new-article-button` - Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard`  ### 6. Published Articles Page - **Route:** `/published` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show published articles filtered by category - **Template:** `published_articles.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Page container: id `published-articles-page` - Category filter dropdown: id `published-category-dropdown` - Articles grid container: id `published-articles-grid` - Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard-published`  ### 7. Calendar Page - **Route:** `/calendar` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show scheduled articles by date and category - **Template:** `calendar.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Page container: id `calendar-page` - Calendar view container: id `calendar-view` - Scheduled articles list container: id `scheduled-articles-list` - Filter dropdown for category: id `calendar-category-dropdown` - Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard-calendar`  ### 8. Analytics Page - **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show article engagement stats - **Template:** `analytics.html`  #### Template structure and element IDs: - Page container: id `analytics-page` - Total views count: id `analytics-total-views` - Unique visitors count: id `analytics-unique-visitors` - Button back to article edit: id `back-to-article-analytics`  ## Data Files and Schemas  ### 1. users.txt - Format: `username|email|full_name|registered_date` - Sample:   - `john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15`   - `bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17`   - `admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10` - Fields:   - username: unique identifier   - email: user email   - full_name: User's full name   - registered_date: YYYY-MM-DD  ### 2. articles.txt - Format:   `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date` - Fields:   - article_id: integer   - title: string   - author: username   - category: enumerated ["news", "press_release", "announcement"]   - status: enumerated ["draft", "pending_review", "approved", "rejected", "published"]   - tags: comma-separated string   - featured_image: URL or empty   - meta_description: string   - created_date: YYYY-MM-DD   - publish_date: YYYY-MM-DD or empty if unpublished - Example:   - `10|Flask Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 Product new` (dates omitted for example)  ### 3. article_versions.txt - Format:   `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|timestamp|notes` - Fields:   - version_id: integer   - article_id: integer   - version_number: integer (incremental)   - content: text of the article version   - author: username who made version   - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS   - notes: string (optional notes about this version) - Example:   - `1|1|1|Flask is a micro framework...|john|2024-01-20 10:00:00|Initial draft`   - `2|1|2|Flask a web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction`  ### 4. approvals.txt - Format:   `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp` - Fields:   - approval_id: integer   - article_id: integer   - version_id: integer   - approver: username   - status: enumerated ["approved", "rejected", "pending"]   - comments: string   - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - Sample:   - `3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more in section|2024-01-23 10:30:00`  ### 5. workflow_stages.txt - Format:   `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required` - Fields:   - stage_id: integer   - category: string   - stage_name: string (e.g., "Approval", "Editor")   - stage_order: integer   - is_required: boolean ("yes" or "no") - Example:   - `2|news|Approval|2|yes`   - `3|news|Editor|3|yes`  ### 6. comments.txt - Format:   `comment_id|article_id|version_id|author|comment_text|timestamp` - Fields:   - comment_id: integer   - article_id: integer   - version_id: integer   - author: username   - comment_text: string   - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - Sample:   - `1|1|1|alice|Great addition|2024-01-21 11:00:00`   - `2|1|2|bob|Needs expansion|2024-01-21 12:00:00`  ### 7. analytics.txt - Format:   `article_id|date|total_views|unique_visitors|avg_time_spent_seconds|bounce_rate` - Fields:   - article_id: integer   - date: YYYY-MM-DD   - total_views: integer   - unique_visitors: integer   - avg_time_spent_seconds: integer   - bounce_rate: integer (percentage) - Example:   - `2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18`  ## Enumerations Summary  - Article Status: `draft`, `pending_review`, `approved`, `rejected`, `published` - Article Categories: `news`, `press_release`, `announcement` - Approval Status: `approved`, `rejected`, `pending`  ---  This concludes the comprehensive design_spec.md including detailed routes with methods, expected templates with exact element IDs, and all data file schemas with field details and examples to enable backend and frontend development without ambiguity.

[DEBUG] Review Content for RequirementsAnalyst -> SystemArchitect:
  [user_task_description] # ContentPublishingHub Web Application Specification  ## Objective Build a comprehensive content management system with version control, content scheduling, and analytics. The system supports collaborative content creation with version history tracking, editorial comments, and content analytics. Testing should start from the Dashboard page.  ## Language Python (Flask framework recommended)  ## Page Design  ### 1. Dashboard Page (`dashboard.html`) **Route:** `/dashboard` **Purpose:** Main landing page showing content overview and navigation **Elements:** - Page container (id: `dashboard-page`) - Welcome message with username (id: `welcome-message`) - Quick stats section (id: `quick-stats`) - Create Article button (id: `create-article-button`) - Recent activity feed (id: `recent-activity`)  ### 2. Create Article Page (`create_article.html`) **Route:** `/article/create` **Purpose:** Editor for creating new articles **Elements:** - Page container (id: `create-article-page`) - Article title input (id: `article-title`) - Content editor textarea (id: `article-content`) - Save as Draft button (id: `save-draft-button`) - Cancel button (id: `cancel-button`)  ### 3. Edit Article Page (`edit_article.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit` **Purpose:** Edit existing article with version tracking **Elements:** - Page container (id: `edit-article-page`) - Article title input (id: `edit-article-title`) - Content editor textarea (id: `edit-article-content`) - Save New Version button (id: `save-version-button`) - Cancel button (id: `cancel-edit`)  ### 4. Article Version History Page (`version_history.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions` **Purpose:** View all versions and restore previous versions **Elements:** - Page container (id: `version-history-page`) - Versions list (id: `versions-list`) - Version comparison section (id: `version-comparison`) - Restore button (id: `restore-version-1`) - Back to Edit button (id: `back-to-edit-history`)  ### 5. My Articles Page (`my_articles.html`) **Route:** `/articles/mine` **Purpose:** List user's articles with filters **Elements:** - Page container (id: `my-articles-page`) - Filter by status dropdown (id: `filter-article-status`) - Articles table (id: `articles-table`) - Create New Article button (id: `create-new-article`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard`)  ### 6. Published Articles Page (`published_articles.html`) **Route:** `/articles/published` **Purpose:** Public-facing content library **Elements:** - Page container (id: `published-articles-page`) - Filter by category dropdown (id: `filter-published-category`) - Articles grid (id: `published-articles-grid`) - Sort by dropdown (id: `sort-published`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-published`)  ### 7. Content Calendar Page (`content_calendar.html`) **Route:** `/calendar` **Purpose:** Scheduled publications timeline view **Elements:** - Page container (id: `calendar-page`) - Calendar view selector (id: `calendar-view`) - Calendar grid (id: `calendar-grid`) - Schedule button (id: `schedule-button`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`)  ### 8. Article Analytics Page (`article_analytics.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics` **Purpose:** View engagement metrics for published article **Elements:** - Page container (id: `analytics-page`) - Analytics overview (id: `analytics-overview`) - Total views (id: `analytics-total-views`) - Unique visitors (id: `analytics-unique-visitors`) - Back to Article button (id: `back-to-article-analytics`)  ## Data Storage  The 'ContentPublishingHub' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. users.txt Format: `username|email|fullname|created_date` Example: ``` john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15 alice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16 bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17 admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10 ```  ### 2. articles.txt Format: `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date` Example: ``` 1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00 2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00 3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24| ``` status: draft, pending_review, under_review, approved, published, rejected, archived category: news, blog, tutorial, announcement, press_release  ### 3. article_versions.txt Format: `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary` Example: ``` 1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft 2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction 3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version ```  ### 4. approvals.txt Format: `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp` Example: ``` 1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00 2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00 3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00 ``` status: approved, rejected, revision_requested  ### 5. workflow_stages.txt Format: `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required` Example: ``` 1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes 2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes 3|news|Editor Review|1|yes 4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes 5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes ```  ### 6. comments.txt Format: `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp` Example: ``` 1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00 2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00 3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00 ```  ### 7. analytics.txt Format: `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares` Example: ``` 1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12 2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18 3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15 4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8 ```
  [design_spec.md] # ContentPublishingHub - Design Specification for Provided Web Application  ## Flask Routes, Templates, and Elements  ### 1. Dashboard Page - **Route:** `/dashboard` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Home page with user message and recent activity - **Template:** `dashboard.html`  #### Template elements (IDs): - Page container: `dashboard-page` - Message display: `dashboard-message` - Create Article button: `create-article-button` - Recent activity section: `recent-activity`  ### 2. Create Article Page - **Route:** `/article/create` - **Methods:** GET, POST - **Purpose:** Create new article - **Template:** `create_article.html`  #### Template elements (IDs): - Page container: `create-article-page` - Article editor (textarea or WYSIWYG): `article-editor` - Save Draft button: `save-draft-button` - Cancel button: `cancel-button`  ### 3. Edit Article Page - **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit` - **Methods:** GET, POST - **Purpose:** Edit an existing article and save version - **Template:** `edit_article.html`  #### Template elements (IDs): - Page container: `edit-article-page` - Title input field: `edit-article-title` - Content textarea/editor: `edit-article-content` - Save Version button: `save-version-button` - Cancel button (if applicable): `cancel-button`  ### 4. Version History Page - **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show versions history, compare, and restore - **Template:** `version_history.html`  #### Template elements (IDs): - Page container: `version-history-page` - Versions list container: `versions-list` - Version comparison container: `version-comparison` - Restore button for version 1: `restore-version-1` (similar pattern for others) - Back to article button: `back-to-edit-article-button`  ### 5. User's Articles Page - **Route:** `/articles/mine` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** List and filter logged-in user's articles - **Template:** `my_articles.html`  #### Template elements (IDs): - Page container: `my-articles-page` - Status filter dropdown: `filter-article-status` - Articles table/grid: `articles-table` - Create new article button: `new-article-button` - Back to dashboard button: `back-to-dashboard`  ### 6. Published Articles Page - **Route:** `/articles/published` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Public-facing article library - **Template:** `published_articles.html`  #### Template elements (IDs): - Page container: `published-articles-page` - Category filter dropdown: `filter-published-category` - Articles grid container: `published-articles-grid` - Sort dropdown: `sort-published` - Back button: `back-to-dashboard-published`  ### 7. Calendar Page - **Route:** `/calendar` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Scheduled articles timeline and calendar view - **Template:** `calendar.html`  #### Template elements (IDs): - Page container: `calendar-page` - Calendar grid: `calendar-grid` - Schedule button: `schedule-button` - Back to dashboard button: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`  ### 8. Analytics Page - **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** View article analytics data - **Template:** `article_analytics.html`  #### Template elements (IDs): - Page container: `analytics-page` - Views statistic: `analytics-views` - Visitors statistic: `analytics-visitors` - Back button: `back-to-dashboard-analytics`   ## Data Storage Files and Schemas  ### users.txt - Format (pipe-delimited):   `username|email|fullname|registered_date` - Example: ```  john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15 admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10 ```  ### articles.txt - Format:   `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date` - Categories: `tutorial`, `news`, `press_release`, `announcement` - Statuses: `draft`, `under_review`, `published` - Example: ```  1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-15|2024-01-24 2|Company Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-23| ```  ### article_versions.txt - Format:   `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|timestamp|notes` - Example: ``` 1|1|1|Flask is a micro framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft 2|1|2|Improved introduction...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Added details ```  ### approvals.txt - Format:   `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp` - Statuses: `approved`, `rejected`, `pending` - Example: ``` 1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good|2024-01-22 16:00:00 2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publish|2024-01-23 13:45:00 ```  ### workflow_stages.txt - Format:   `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required` - Example: ``` 3|news|Editor Review|1|yes 4|announcement|Editor|2|yes ```  ### comments.txt - Format:   `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp` - Example: ``` 3|3|1|alice|Section expansion needed|2024-01-21 13:45:00 ```  ### analytics.txt - Format:   `article_id|date|total_views|unique_visitors|avg_time_spent_seconds|bounce_rate` - Example: ``` 1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12 3|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15 ```  ---  This specification includes all Flask routes with HTTP methods and exact template element ids, and detailed data storage schemas with field order and examples. It aligns with the provided requirements and allows backend/frontend tasks to operate independently and clearly.

=== LLM_call: SystemArchitect ===
INPUT: === user_task_description ===

# ContentPublishingHub Web Application Specification

## Objective
Build a comprehensive content management system with version control, content scheduling, and analytics. The system supports collaborative content creation with version history tracking, editorial comments, and content analytics. Testing should start from the Dashboard page.

## Language
Python (Flask framework recommended)

## Page Design

### 1. Dashboard Page (`dashboard.html`)
**Route:** `/dashboard`
**Purpose:** Main landing page showing content overview and navigation
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `dashboard-page`)
- Welcome message with username (id: `welcome-message`)
- Quick stats section (id: `quick-stats`)
- Create Article button (id: `create-article-button`)
- Recent activity feed (id: `recent-activity`)

### 2. Create Article Page (`create_article.html`)
**Route:** `/article/create`
**Purpose:** Editor for creating new articles
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `create-article-page`)
- Article title input (id: `article-title`)
- Content editor textarea (id: `article-content`)
- Save as Draft button (id: `save-draft-button`)
- Cancel button (id: `cancel-button`)

### 3. Edit Article Page (`edit_article.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit`
**Purpose:** Edit existing article with version tracking
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `edit-article-page`)
- Article title input (id: `edit-article-title`)
- Content editor textarea (id: `edit-article-content`)
- Save New Version button (id: `save-version-button`)
- Cancel button (id: `cancel-edit`)

### 4. Article Version History Page (`version_history.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions`
**Purpose:** View all versions and restore previous versions
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `version-history-page`)
- Versions list (id: `versions-list`)
- Version comparison section (id: `version-comparison`)
- Restore button (id: `restore-version-1`)
- Back to Edit button (id: `back-to-edit-history`)

### 5. My Articles Page (`my_articles.html`)
**Route:** `/articles/mine`
**Purpose:** List user's articles with filters
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `my-articles-page`)
- Filter by status dropdown (id: `filter-article-status`)
- Articles table (id: `articles-table`)
- Create New Article button (id: `create-new-article`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard`)

### 6. Published Articles Page (`published_articles.html`)
**Route:** `/articles/published`
**Purpose:** Public-facing content library
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `published-articles-page`)
- Filter by category dropdown (id: `filter-published-category`)
- Articles grid (id: `published-articles-grid`)
- Sort by dropdown (id: `sort-published`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-published`)

### 7. Content Calendar Page (`content_calendar.html`)
**Route:** `/calendar`
**Purpose:** Scheduled publications timeline view
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `calendar-page`)
- Calendar view selector (id: `calendar-view`)
- Calendar grid (id: `calendar-grid`)
- Schedule button (id: `schedule-button`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`)

### 8. Article Analytics Page (`article_analytics.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics`
**Purpose:** View engagement metrics for published article
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `analytics-page`)
- Analytics overview (id: `analytics-overview`)
- Total views (id: `analytics-total-views`)
- Unique visitors (id: `analytics-unique-visitors`)
- Back to Article button (id: `back-to-article-analytics`)

## Data Storage

The 'ContentPublishingHub' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. users.txt
Format: `username|email|fullname|created_date`
Example:
```
john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15
alice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16
bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17
admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10
```

### 2. articles.txt
Format: `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date`
Example:
```
1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00
2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00
3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24|
```
status: draft, pending_review, under_review, approved, published, rejected, archived
category: news, blog, tutorial, announcement, press_release

### 3. article_versions.txt
Format: `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary`
Example:
```
1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft
2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction
3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version
```

### 4. approvals.txt
Format: `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp`
Example:
```
1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00
2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00
3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00
```
status: approved, rejected, revision_requested

### 5. workflow_stages.txt
Format: `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required`
Example:
```
1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes
2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes
3|news|Editor Review|1|yes
4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes
5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes
```

### 6. comments.txt
Format: `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp`
Example:
```
1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00
2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00
3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00
```

### 7. analytics.txt
Format: `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares`
Example:
```
1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12
2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18
3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15
4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8
```


=== design_spec.md ===
# ContentPublishingHub - Design Specification for Provided Web Application

## Flask Routes, Templates, and Elements

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Route:** `/dashboard`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Home page with user message and recent activity
- **Template:** `dashboard.html`

#### Template elements (IDs):
- Page container: `dashboard-page`
- Message display: `dashboard-message`
- Create Article button: `create-article-button`
- Recent activity section: `recent-activity`

### 2. Create Article Page
- **Route:** `/article/create`
- **Methods:** GET, POST
- **Purpose:** Create new article
- **Template:** `create_article.html`

#### Template elements (IDs):
- Page container: `create-article-page`
- Article editor (textarea or WYSIWYG): `article-editor`
- Save Draft button: `save-draft-button`
- Cancel button: `cancel-button`

### 3. Edit Article Page
- **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit`
- **Methods:** GET, POST
- **Purpose:** Edit an existing article and save version
- **Template:** `edit_article.html`

#### Template elements (IDs):
- Page container: `edit-article-page`
- Title input field: `edit-article-title`
- Content textarea/editor: `edit-article-content`
- Save Version button: `save-version-button`
- Cancel button (if applicable): `cancel-button`

### 4. Version History Page
- **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show versions history, compare, and restore
- **Template:** `version_history.html`

#### Template elements (IDs):
- Page container: `version-history-page`
- Versions list container: `versions-list`
- Version comparison container: `version-comparison`
- Restore button for version 1: `restore-version-1` (similar pattern for others)
- Back to article button: `back-to-edit-article-button`

### 5. User's Articles Page
- **Route:** `/articles/mine`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** List and filter logged-in user's articles
- **Template:** `my_articles.html`

#### Template elements (IDs):
- Page container: `my-articles-page`
- Status filter dropdown: `filter-article-status`
- Articles table/grid: `articles-table`
- Create new article button: `new-article-button`
- Back to dashboard button: `back-to-dashboard`

### 6. Published Articles Page
- **Route:** `/articles/published`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Public-facing article library
- **Template:** `published_articles.html`

#### Template elements (IDs):
- Page container: `published-articles-page`
- Category filter dropdown: `filter-published-category`
- Articles grid container: `published-articles-grid`
- Sort dropdown: `sort-published`
- Back button: `back-to-dashboard-published`

### 7. Calendar Page
- **Route:** `/calendar`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Scheduled articles timeline and calendar view
- **Template:** `calendar.html`

#### Template elements (IDs):
- Page container: `calendar-page`
- Calendar grid: `calendar-grid`
- Schedule button: `schedule-button`
- Back to dashboard button: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`

### 8. Analytics Page
- **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** View article analytics data
- **Template:** `article_analytics.html`

#### Template elements (IDs):
- Page container: `analytics-page`
- Views statistic: `analytics-views`
- Visitors statistic: `analytics-visitors`
- Back button: `back-to-dashboard-analytics`


## Data Storage Files and Schemas

### users.txt
- Format (pipe-delimited):
  `username|email|fullname|registered_date`
- Example:
``` 
john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15
admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10
```

### articles.txt
- Format:
  `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date`
- Categories: `tutorial`, `news`, `press_release`, `announcement`
- Statuses: `draft`, `under_review`, `published`
- Example:
``` 
1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-15|2024-01-24
2|Company Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-23|
```

### article_versions.txt
- Format:
  `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|timestamp|notes`
- Example:
```
1|1|1|Flask is a micro framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft
2|1|2|Improved introduction...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Added details
```

### approvals.txt
- Format:
  `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp`
- Statuses: `approved`, `rejected`, `pending`
- Example:
```
1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good|2024-01-22 16:00:00
2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publish|2024-01-23 13:45:00
```

### workflow_stages.txt
- Format:
  `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required`
- Example:
```
3|news|Editor Review|1|yes
4|announcement|Editor|2|yes
```

### comments.txt
- Format:
  `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp`
- Example:
```
3|3|1|alice|Section expansion needed|2024-01-21 13:45:00
```

### analytics.txt
- Format:
  `article_id|date|total_views|unique_visitors|avg_time_spent_seconds|bounce_rate`
- Example:
```
1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12
3|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15
```

This specification includes all Flask routes with HTTP methods and exact template element ids, and detailed data storage schemas with field order and examples. It aligns with the provided requirements and allows backend/frontend tasks to operate independently and clearly.

Format:   `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required` - Example: ``` 3|news|Editor Review|1|yes 4|announcement|Editor|2|yes ```  ### comments.txt - Format:   `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp` - Example: ``` 3|3|1|alice|Section expansion needed|2024-01-21 13:45:00 ```  ### analytics.txt - Format:   `article_id|date|total_views|unique_visitors|avg_time_spent_seconds|bounce_rate` - Example: ``` 1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12 3|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15 ```  ---  This specification includes all Flask routes with HTTP methods and exact template element ids, and detailed data storage schemas with field order and examples. It aligns with the provided requirements and allows backend/frontend tasks to operate independently and clearly.

# ContentPublishingHub Web Application Specification

## Objective
Build a comprehensive content management system with version control, content scheduling, and analytics. The system supports collaborative content creation with version history tracking, editorial comments, and content analytics. Testing should start from the Dashboard page.

## Language
Python (Flask framework recommended)

## Page Design

### 1. Dashboard Page (`dashboard.html`)
**Route:** `/dashboard`
**Purpose:** Main landing page showing content overview and navigation
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `dashboard-page`)
- Welcome message with username (id: `welcome-message`)
- Quick stats section (id: `quick-stats`)
- Create Article button (id: `create-article-button`)
- Recent activity feed (id: `recent-activity`)

### 2. Create Article Page (`create_article.html`)
**Route:** `/article/create`
**Purpose:** Editor for creating new articles
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `create-article-page`)
- Article title input (id: `article-title`)
- Content editor textarea (id: `article-content`)
- Save as Draft button (id: `save-draft-button`)
- Cancel button (id: `cancel-button`)

### 3. Edit Article Page (`edit_article.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit`
**Purpose:** Edit existing article with version tracking
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `edit-article-page`)
- Article title input (id: `edit-article-title`)
- Content editor textarea (id: `edit-article-content`)
- Save New Version button (id: `save-version-button`)
- Cancel button (id: `cancel-edit`)

### 4. Article Version History Page (`version_history.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions`
**Purpose:** View all versions and restore previous versions
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `version-history-page`)
- Versions list (id: `versions-list`)
- Version comparison section (id: `version-comparison`)
- Restore button (id: `restore-version-1`)
- Back to Edit button (id: `back-to-edit-history`)

### 5. My Articles Page (`my_articles.html`)
**Route:** `/articles/mine`
**Purpose:** List user's articles with filters
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `my-articles-page`)
- Filter by status dropdown (id: `filter-article-status`)
- Articles table (id: `articles-table`)
- Create New Article button (id: `create-new-article`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard`)

### 6. Published Articles Page (`published_articles.html`)
**Route:** `/articles/published`
**Purpose:** Public-facing content library
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `published-articles-page`)
- Filter by category dropdown (id: `filter-published-category`)
- Articles grid (id: `published-articles-grid`)
- Sort by dropdown (id: `sort-published`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-published`)

### 7. Content Calendar Page (`content_calendar.html`)
**Route:** `/calendar`
**Purpose:** Scheduled publications timeline view
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `calendar-page`)
- Calendar view selector (id: `calendar-view`)
- Calendar grid (id: `calendar-grid`)
- Schedule button (id: `schedule-button`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`)

### 8. Article Analytics Page (`article_analytics.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics`
**Purpose:** View engagement metrics for published article
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `analytics-page`)
- Analytics overview (id: `analytics-overview`)
- Total views (id: `analytics-total-views`)
- Unique visitors (id: `analytics-unique-visitors`)
- Back to Article button (id: `back-to-article-analytics`)

## Data Storage

The 'ContentPublishingHub' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. users.txt
Format: `username|email|fullname|created_date`
Example:
```
john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15
alice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16
bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17
admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10
```

### 2. articles.txt
Format: `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date`
Example:
```
1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00
2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00
3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24|
```
status: draft, pending_review, under_review, approved, published, rejected, archived
category: news, blog, tutorial, announcement, press_release

### 3. article_versions.txt
Format: `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary`
Example:
```
1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft
2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction
3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version
```

### 4. approvals.txt
Format: `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp`
Example:
```
1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00
2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00
3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00
```
status: approved, rejected, revision_requested

### 5. workflow_stages.txt
Format: `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required`
Example:
```
1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes
2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes
3|news|Editor Review|1|yes
4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes
5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes
```

### 6. comments.txt
Format: `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp`
Example:
```
1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00
2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00
3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00
```

### 7. analytics.txt
Format: `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares`
Example:
```
1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12
2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18
3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15
4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8
```
