Edit Article Page\n- **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit`\n- **Methods:** GET, POST\n- **Purpose:** Edit an existing article and manage versions\n- **Template:** `edit_article.html`\n\n#### Template structure and element IDs:\n- Page container div: id `edit-article-page`\n- Article title input (text): id `edit-article-title`\n- Article content textarea: id `edit-article-content`\n- Save changes button: id `edit-save-button`\n- View version history button: id `version-history-button`\n\n### 4. Version History Page\n- **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions`\n- **Methods:** GET\n- **Purpose:** View versions of an article and restore if needed\n- **Template:** `version_history.html`\n\n#### Template structure and element IDs:\n- Page container: id `version-history-page`\n- List of versions container: id `version-list`\n- Each version entry: div with id `version-{version_id}`\n- Restore button for version 1: id `restore-version-1` (similar pattern for others e.g. `restore-version-{version_id}`)\n\n### 5. My Articles Page\n- **Route:** `/my-articles`\n- **Methods:** GET\n- **Purpose:** List articles belonging to logged in user with filter\n- **Template:** `my_articles.html`\n\n#### Template structure and element IDs:\n- Page container: id `my-articles-page`\n- Filter dropdown for status: id `filter-status-dropdown`\n- Filter dropdown for category: id `filter-category-dropdown`\n- List/grid container for articles: id `user-articles-list`\n- Create new article button: id `create-new-article-button`\n- Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard`\n\n### 6. Published Articles Page\n- **Route:** `/published`\n- **Methods:** GET\n- **Purpose:** Show published articles filtered by category\n- **Template:** `published_articles.html`\n\n#### Template structure and element IDs:\n- Page container: id `published-articles-page`\n- Category filter dropdown: id `published-category-dropdown`\n- Articles grid container: id `published-articles-grid`\n- Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard-published`\n\n### 7. Calendar Page\n- **Route:** `/calendar`\n- **Methods:** GET\n- **Purpose:** Show scheduled articles by date and category\n- **Template:** `calendar.html`\n\n#### Template structure and element IDs:\n- Page container: id `calendar-page`\n- Calendar view container: id `calendar-view`\n- Scheduled articles list container: id `scheduled-articles-list`\n- Filter dropdown for category: id `calendar-category-dropdown`\n- Back to dashboard button: id `back-to-dashboard-calendar`\n\n### 8. Analytics Page\n- **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics`\n- **Methods:** GET\n- **Purpose:** Show article engagement stats\n- **Template:** `analytics.html`\n\n#### Template structure and element IDs:\n- Page container: id `analytics-page`\n- Total views count: id `analytics-total-views`\n- Unique visitors count: id `analytics-unique-visitors`\n- Button back to article edit: id `back-to-article-analytics`\n\n## Data Files and Schemas\n\n### 1. users.txt\n- Format: `username|email|full_name|registered_date`\n- Sample:\n  - `john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15`\n  - `bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17`\n  - `admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10`\n- Fields:\n  - username: unique identifier\n  - email: user email\n  - full_name: User's full name\n  - registered_date: YYYY-MM-DD\n\n### 2. articles.txt\n- Format:\n  `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date`\n- Fields:\n  - article_id: integer\n  - title: string\n  - author: username\n  - category: enumerated [\"news\", \"press_release\", \"announcement\"]\n  - status: enumerated [\"draft\", \"pending_review\", \"approved\", \"rejected\", \"published\"]\n  - tags: comma-separated string\n  - featured_image: URL or empty\n  - meta_description: string\n  - created_date: YYYY-MM-DD\n  - publish_date: YYYY-MM-DD or empty if unpublished\n- Example:\n  - `10|Flask Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 Product new` (dates omitted for example)\n\n### 3. article_versions.txt\n- Format:\n  `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|timestamp|notes`\n- Fields:\n  - version_id: integer\n  - article_id: integer\n  - version_number: integer (incremental)\n  - content: text of the article version\n  - author: username who made version\n  - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS\n  - notes: string (optional notes about this version)\n- Example:\n  - `1|1|1|Flask is a micro framework...|john|2024-01-20 10:00:00|Initial draft`\n  - `2|1|2|Flask a web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction`\n\n### 4. approvals.txt\n- Format:\n  `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp`\n- Fields:\n  - approval_id: integer\n  - article_id: integer\n  - version_id: integer\n  - approver: username\n  - status: enumerated [\"approved\", \"rejected\", \"pending\"]\n  - comments: string\n  - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS\n- Sample:\n  - `3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more in section|2024-01-23 10:30:00`\n\n### 5. workflow_stages.txt\n- Format:\n  `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required`\n- Fields:\n  - stage_id: integer\n  - category: string\n  - stage_name: string (e.g., \"Approval\", \"Editor\")\n  - stage_order: integer\n  - is_required: boolean (\"yes\" or \"no\")\n- Example:\n  - `2|news|Approval|2|yes`\n  - `3|news|Editor|3|yes`\n\n### 6. comments.txt\n- Format:\n  `comment_id|article_id|version_id|author|comment_text|timestamp`\n- Fields:\n  - comment_id: integer\n  - article_id: integer\n  - version_id: integer\n  - author: username\n  - comment_text: string\n  - timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS\n- Sample:\n  - `1|1|1|alice|Great addition|2024-01-21 11:00:00`\n  - `2|1|2|bob|Needs expansion|2024-01-21 12:00:00`\n\n### 7. analytics.txt\n- Format:\n  `article_id|date|total_views|unique_visitors|avg_time_spent_seconds|bounce_rate`\n- Fields:\n  - article_id: integer\n  - date: YYYY-MM-DD\n  - total_views: integer\n  - unique_visitors: integer\n  - avg_time_spent_seconds: integer\n  - bounce_rate: integer (percentage)\n- Example:\n  - `2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18`\n\n## Enumerations Summary\n\n- Article Status: `draft`, `pending_review`, `approved`, `rejected`, `published`\n- Article Categories: `news`, `press_release`, `announcement`\n- Approval Status: `approved`, `rejected`, `pending`\n\n---\n\nThis concludes the comprehensive design_spec.md including detailed routes with methods, expected templates with exact element IDs, and all data file schemas with field details and examples to enable backend and frontend development without ambiguity."})

# ContentPublishingHub Web Application Specification

## Objective
Build a comprehensive content management system with version control, content scheduling, and analytics. The system supports collaborative content creation with version history tracking, editorial comments, and content analytics. Testing should start from the Dashboard page.

## Language
Python (Flask framework recommended)

## Page Design

### 1. Dashboard Page (`dashboard.html`)
**Route:** `/dashboard`
**Purpose:** Main landing page showing content overview and navigation
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `dashboard-page`)
- Welcome message with username (id: `welcome-message`)
- Quick stats section (id: `quick-stats`)
- Create Article button (id: `create-article-button`)
- Recent activity feed (id: `recent-activity`)

### 2. Create Article Page (`create_article.html`)
**Route:** `/article/create`
**Purpose:** Editor for creating new articles
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `create-article-page`)
- Article title input (id: `article-title`)
- Content editor textarea (id: `article-content`)
- Save as Draft button (id: `save-draft-button`)
- Cancel button (id: `cancel-button`)

### 3. Edit Article Page (`edit_article.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit`
**Purpose:** Edit existing article with version tracking
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `edit-article-page`)
- Article title input (id: `edit-article-title`)
- Content editor textarea (id: `edit-article-content`)
- Save New Version button (id: `save-version-button`)
- Cancel button (id: `cancel-edit`)

### 4. Article Version History Page (`version_history.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions`
**Purpose:** View all versions and restore previous versions
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `version-history-page`)
- Versions list (id: `versions-list`)
- Version comparison section (id: `version-comparison`)
- Restore button (id: `restore-version-1`)
- Back to Edit button (id: `back-to-edit-history`)

### 5. My Articles Page (`my_articles.html`)
**Route:** `/articles/mine`
**Purpose:** List user's articles with filters
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `my-articles-page`)
- Filter by status dropdown (id: `filter-article-status`)
- Articles table (id: `articles-table`)
- Create New Article button (id: `create-new-article`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard`)

### 6. Published Articles Page (`published_articles.html`)
**Route:** `/articles/published`
**Purpose:** Public-facing content library
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `published-articles-page`)
- Filter by category dropdown (id: `filter-published-category`)
- Articles grid (id: `published-articles-grid`)
- Sort by dropdown (id: `sort-published`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-published`)

### 7. Content Calendar Page (`content_calendar.html`)
**Route:** `/calendar`
**Purpose:** Scheduled publications timeline view
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `calendar-page`)
- Calendar view selector (id: `calendar-view`)
- Calendar grid (id: `calendar-grid`)
- Schedule button (id: `schedule-button`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`)

### 8. Article Analytics Page (`article_analytics.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics`
**Purpose:** View engagement metrics for published article
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `analytics-page`)
- Analytics overview (id: `analytics-overview`)
- Total views (id: `analytics-total-views`)
- Unique visitors (id: `analytics-unique-visitors`)
- Back to Article button (id: `back-to-article-analytics`)

## Data Storage

The 'ContentPublishingHub' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. users.txt
Format: `username|email|fullname|created_date`
Example:
```
john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15
alice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16
bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17
admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10
```

### 2. articles.txt
Format: `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date`
Example:
```
1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00
2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00
3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24|
```
status: draft, pending_review, under_review, approved, published, rejected, archived
category: news, blog, tutorial, announcement, press_release

### 3. article_versions.txt
Format: `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary`
Example:
```
1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft
2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction
3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version
```

### 4. approvals.txt
Format: `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp`
Example:
```
1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00
2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00
3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00
```
status: approved, rejected, revision_requested

### 5. workflow_stages.txt
Format: `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required`
Example:
```
1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes
2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes
3|news|Editor Review|1|yes
4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes
5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes
```

### 6. comments.txt
Format: `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp`
Example:
```
1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00
2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00
3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00
```

### 7. analytics.txt
Format: `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares`
Example:
```
1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12
2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18
3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15
4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8
```

`analytics_data` (dict): engagement metrics for article\n\n\n## HTML Templates Specification\n\n### `dashboard.html`\n- Page container id: `dashboard-page`\n- Elements:\n  - Welcome message with id `welcome-message`\n  - Quick stats section id `quick-stats`\n  - Create Article button id `create-article-button` (link to `/article/create`)\n  - Recent activity feed id `recent-activity`\n\n### `create_article.html`\n- Page container id: `create-article-page`\n- Form elements:\n  - Article title input id `article-title`\n  - Content editor textarea id `article-content`\n  - Save as Draft button id `save-draft-button`\n  - Cancel button id `cancel-button` (navigates back or dashboard)\n\n### `edit_article.html`\n- Page container id: `edit-article-page`\n- Form elements:\n  - Article title input id `edit-article-title`\n  - Content editor textarea id `edit-article-content`\n  - Save New Version button id `save-version-button`\n  - Cancel button id `cancel-edit`\n\n### `version_history.html`\n- Page container id: `version-history-page`\n- Versions list container id: `versions-list` (listing all versions)\n- Version comparison section id: `version-comparison`\n- Restore button specifically: `restore-version-1` for version 1 and similar pattern for others\n- Back to Edit button id: `back-to-edit-history`\n\n### `my_articles.html`\n- Page container id: `my-articles-page`\n- Filter dropdown id: `filter-article-status`\n- Articles table id: `articles-table`\n- Create New Article button id: `create-new-article` (links to `/article/create`)\n- Back to Dashboard button id: `back-to-dashboard`\n\n### `published_articles.html`\n- Page container id: `published-articles-page`\n- Filter by category dropdown id: `filter-published-category`\n- Articles grid container id: `published-articles-grid`\n- Sort by dropdown id: `sort-published`\n- Back to Dashboard button id: `back-to-dashboard-published`\n\n### `content_calendar.html`\n- Page container id: `calendar-page`\n- Calendar view selector dropdown id: `calendar-view`\n- Calendar grid id: `calendar-grid`\n- Schedule button id: `schedule-button`\n- Back to Dashboard button id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`\n\n### `article_analytics.html`\n- Page container id: `analytics-page`\n- Analytics overview section id: `analytics-overview`\n- Total views display id: `analytics-total-views`\n- Unique visitors display id: `analytics-unique-visitors`\n- Back to Article button id: `back-to-article-analytics`\n\n\n## Data File Schemas Specification\n\n### 1. users.txt\n- Pipe-delimited fields:\n  `username|email|fullname|created_date`\n- Meaning:\n  - username: unique user identifier (string)\n  - email: user email address (string)\n  - fullname: full name of user (string)\n  - created_date: account creation date (YYYY-MM-DD string)\n- Example rows:\n  ```\njohn|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15\nalice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16\nbob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17\nadmin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10\n```\n\n### 2. articles.txt\n- Pipe-delimited fields:\n  `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date`\n- Meaning:\n  - article_id: integer unique article ID\n  - title: article title string\n  - author: username string\n  - category: enumerated string values [\"news\", \"blog\", \"tutorial\", \"announcement\", \"press_release\"]\n  - status: enumerated string values [\"draft\", \"pending_review\", \"under_review\", \"approved\", \"published\", \"rejected\", \"archived\"]\n  - tags: comma-separated string list\n  - featured_image: URL path string or empty\n  - meta_description: short description string\n  - created_date: date string YYYY-MM-DD\n  - publish_date: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS or empty if unpublished\n- Example rows:\n  ```\n1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00\n2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00\n3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24|\n```\n\n### 3. article_versions.txt\n- Pipe-delimited fields:\n  `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary`\n- Meaning:\n  - version_id: unique integer version id\n  - article_id: integer article ID\n  - version_number: integer (incrementing version number)\n  - content: string with full article text\n  - author: username string\n  - created_date: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS\n  - change_summary: brief string description of changes\n- Example rows:\n  ```\n1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft\n2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction\n3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version\n```\n\n### 4. approvals.txt\n- Pipe-delimited fields:\n  `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp`\n- Meaning:\n  - approval_id: unique integer id\n  - article_id: integer article ID\n  - version_id: integer article version ID\n  - approver: username string\n  - status: enumerated string [\"approved\", \"rejected\", \"revision_requested\"]\n  - comments: free text comments string\n  - timestamp: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS\n- Example rows:\n  ```\n1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00\n2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00\n3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00\n```\n\n### 5. workflow_stages.txt\n- Pipe-delimited fields:\n  `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required`\n- Meaning:\n  - stage_id: unique integer stage identifier\n  - category: article category string\n  - stage_name: name of workflow stage string\n  - stage_order: integer order of workflow stages\n  - is_required: string \"yes\" or \"no\" indicating if stage is mandatory\n- Example rows:\n  ```\n1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes\n2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes\n3|news|Editor Review|1|yes\n4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes\n5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes\n```\n\n### 6. comments.txt\n- Pipe-delimited fields:\n  `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp`\n- Meaning:\n  - comment_id: unique integer id\n  - article_id: integer article ID\n  - version_id: integer article version ID\n  - user: username string\n  - comment_text: comment content string\n  - timestamp: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS\n- Example rows:\n  ```\n1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00\n2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00\n3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00\n```\n\n### 7. analytics.txt\n- Pipe-delimited fields:\n  `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares`\n- Meaning:\n  - analytics_id: unique integer id\n  - article_id: integer\n  - date: YYYY-MM-DD\n  - views: integer total page views\n  - unique_visitors: integer unique visitors\n  - avg_time_seconds: integer average duration of visit in seconds\n  - shares: integer social shares count\n- Example rows:\n  ```\n1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12\n2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18\n3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15\n4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8\n```\n\n---\n\nThis design specification document fully aligns with user requirements to enable backend and frontend development concurrently and independently."})

  [user_task_description] # ContentPublishingHub Web Application Specification  ## Objective Build a comprehensive content management system with version control, content scheduling, and analytics. The system supports collaborative content creation with version history tracking, editorial comments, and content analytics. Testing should start from the Dashboard page.  ## Language Python (Flask framework recommended)  ## Page Design  ### 1. Dashboard Page (`dashboard.html`) **Route:** `/dashboard` **Purpose:** Main landing page showing content overview and navigation **Elements:** - Page container (id: `dashboard-page`) - Welcome message with username (id: `welcome-message`) - Quick stats section (id: `quick-stats`) - Create Article button (id: `create-article-button`) - Recent activity feed (id: `recent-activity`)  ### 2. Create Article Page (`create_article.html`) **Route:** `/article/create` **Purpose:** Editor for creating new articles **Elements:** - Page container (id: `create-article-page`) - Article title input (id: `article-title`) - Content editor textarea (id: `article-content`) - Save as Draft button (id: `save-draft-button`) - Cancel button (id: `cancel-button`)  ### 3. Edit Article Page (`edit_article.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit` **Purpose:** Edit existing article with version tracking **Elements:** - Page container (id: `edit-article-page`) - Article title input (id: `edit-article-title`) - Content editor textarea (id: `edit-article-content`) - Save New Version button (id: `save-version-button`) - Cancel button (id: `cancel-edit`)  ### 4. Article Version History Page (`version_history.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions` **Purpose:** View all versions and restore previous versions **Elements:** - Page container (id: `version-history-page`) - Versions list (id: `versions-list`) - Version comparison section (id: `version-comparison`) - Restore button (id: `restore-version-1`) - Back to Edit button (id: `back-to-edit-history`)  ### 5. My Articles Page (`my_articles.html`) **Route:** `/articles/mine` **Purpose:** List user's articles with filters **Elements:** - Page container (id: `my-articles-page`) - Filter by status dropdown (id: `filter-article-status`) - Articles table (id: `articles-table`) - Create New Article button (id: `create-new-article`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard`)  ### 6. Published Articles Page (`published_articles.html`) **Route:** `/articles/published` **Purpose:** Public-facing content library **Elements:** - Page container (id: `published-articles-page`) - Filter by category dropdown (id: `filter-published-category`) - Articles grid (id: `published-articles-grid`) - Sort by dropdown (id: `sort-published`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-published`)  ### 7. Content Calendar Page (`content_calendar.html`) **Route:** `/calendar` **Purpose:** Scheduled publications timeline view **Elements:** - Page container (id: `calendar-page`) - Calendar view selector (id: `calendar-view`) - Calendar grid (id: `calendar-grid`) - Schedule button (id: `schedule-button`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`)  ### 8. Article Analytics Page (`article_analytics.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics` **Purpose:** View engagement metrics for published article **Elements:** - Page container (id: `analytics-page`) - Analytics overview (id: `analytics-overview`) - Total views (id: `analytics-total-views`) - Unique visitors (id: `analytics-unique-visitors`) - Back to Article button (id: `back-to-article-analytics`)  ## Data Storage  The 'ContentPublishingHub' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. users.txt Format: `username|email|fullname|created_date` Example: ``` john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15 alice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16 bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17 admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10 ```  ### 2. articles.txt Format: `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date` Example: ``` 1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00 2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00 3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24| ``` status: draft, pending_review, under_review, approved, published, rejected, archived category: news, blog, tutorial, announcement, press_release  ### 3. article_versions.txt Format: `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary` Example: ``` 1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft 2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction 3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version ```  ### 4. approvals.txt Format: `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp` Example: ``` 1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00 2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00 3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00 ``` status: approved, rejected, revision_requested  ### 5. workflow_stages.txt Format: `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required` Example: ``` 1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes 2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes 3|news|Editor Review|1|yes 4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes 5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes ```  ### 6. comments.txt Format: `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp` Example: ``` 1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00 2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00 3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00 ```  ### 7. analytics.txt Format: `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares` Example: ``` 1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12 2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18 3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15 4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8 ```
  [design_spec.md] # ContentPublishingHub Design Specification  ## Flask Routes Specification  ### 1. Dashboard Page - Route URL: `/dashboard` - HTTP Methods: GET - Function Handler Name: `dashboard` - Template Rendered: `dashboard.html` - Context Variables to Template:   - `username` (str): current logged-in user's name   - `quick_stats` (dict): statistics summary   - `recent_activity` (list): recent user activity entries  ### 2. Create Article Page - Route URL: `/article/create` - HTTP Methods: GET, POST - Function Handler Name: `create_article` - Template Rendered: `create_article.html` - Context Variables:   - For GET: none or blank form   - For POST: validation errors, success messages  ### 3. Edit Article Page - Route URL: `/article/<article_id>/edit` - HTTP Methods: GET, POST - Function Handler Name: `edit_article` - Template Rendered: `edit_article.html` - Context Variables:   - `article` (dict): article details including current title and content  ### 4. Article Version History Page - Route URL: `/article/<article_id>/versions` - HTTP Methods: GET - Function Handler Name: `version_history` - Template Rendered: `version_history.html` - Context Variables:   - `versions` (list): list of versions with metadata for the article   - `current_version_id` (int)  ### 5. My Articles Page - Route URL: `/articles/mine` - HTTP Methods: GET - Function Handler Name: `my_articles` - Template Rendered: `my_articles.html` - Context Variables:   - `articles` (list): user's articles filtered by status   - `filter_status` (str): current filter status  ### 6. Published Articles Page - Route URL: `/articles/published` - HTTP Methods: GET - Function Handler Name: `published_articles` - Template Rendered: `published_articles.html` - Context Variables:   - `articles` (list): published articles filtered by category and sort   - `filter_category` (str)   - `sort_order` (str)  ### 7. Content Calendar Page - Route URL: `/calendar` - HTTP Methods: GET - Function Handler Name: `content_calendar` - Template Rendered: `content_calendar.html` - Context Variables:   - `calendar_view` (str): current selected calendar view   - `scheduled_articles` (list): scheduled articles for display  ### 8. Article Analytics Page - Route URL: `/article/<article_id>/analytics` - HTTP Methods: GET - Function Handler Name: `article_analytics` - Template Rendered: `article_analytics.html` - Context Variables:   - `analytics_data` (dict): engagement metrics for article   ## HTML Templates Specification  ### `dashboard.html` - Page container id: `dashboard-page` - Elements:   - Welcome message with id `welcome-message`   - Quick stats section id `quick-stats`   - Create Article button id `create-article-button` (link to `/article/create`)   - Recent activity feed id `recent-activity`  ### `create_article.html` - Page container id: `create-article-page` - Form elements:   - Article title input id `article-title`   - Content editor textarea id `article-content`   - Save as Draft button id `save-draft-button`   - Cancel button id `cancel-button` (navigates back or dashboard)  ### `edit_article.html` - Page container id: `edit-article-page` - Form elements:   - Article title input id `edit-article-title`   - Content editor textarea id `edit-article-content`   - Save New Version button id `save-version-button`   - Cancel button id `cancel-edit`  ### `version_history.html` - Page container id: `version-history-page` - Versions list container id: `versions-list` (listing all versions) - Version comparison section id: `version-comparison` - Restore button specifically: `restore-version-1` for version 1 and similar pattern for others - Back to Edit button id: `back-to-edit-history`  ### `my_articles.html` - Page container id: `my-articles-page` - Filter dropdown id: `filter-article-status` - Articles table id: `articles-table` - Create New Article button id: `create-new-article` (links to `/article/create`) - Back to Dashboard button id: `back-to-dashboard`  ### `published_articles.html` - Page container id: `published-articles-page` - Filter by category dropdown id: `filter-published-category` - Articles grid container id: `published-articles-grid` - Sort by dropdown id: `sort-published` - Back to Dashboard button id: `back-to-dashboard-published`  ### `content_calendar.html` - Page container id: `calendar-page` - Calendar view selector dropdown id: `calendar-view` - Calendar grid id: `calendar-grid` - Schedule button id: `schedule-button` - Back to Dashboard button id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`  ### `article_analytics.html` - Page container id: `analytics-page` - Analytics overview section id: `analytics-overview` - Total views display id: `analytics-total-views` - Unique visitors display id: `analytics-unique-visitors` - Back to Article button id: `back-to-article-analytics`   ## Data File Schemas Specification  ### 1. users.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `username|email|fullname|created_date` - Meaning:   - username: unique user identifier (string)   - email: user email address (string)   - fullname: full name of user (string)   - created_date: account creation date (YYYY-MM-DD string) - Example rows:   ``` john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15 alice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16 bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17 admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10 ```  ### 2. articles.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date` - Meaning:   - article_id: integer unique article ID   - title: article title string   - author: username string   - category: enumerated string values ["news", "blog", "tutorial", "announcement", "press_release"]   - status: enumerated string values ["draft", "pending_review", "under_review", "approved", "published", "rejected", "archived"]   - tags: comma-separated string list   - featured_image: URL path string or empty   - meta_description: short description string   - created_date: date string YYYY-MM-DD   - publish_date: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS or empty if unpublished - Example rows:   ``` 1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00 2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00 3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24| ```  ### 3. article_versions.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary` - Meaning:   - version_id: unique integer version id   - article_id: integer article ID   - version_number: integer (incrementing version number)   - content: string with full article text   - author: username string   - created_date: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS   - change_summary: brief string description of changes - Example rows:   ``` 1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft 2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction 3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version ```  ### 4. approvals.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp` - Meaning:   - approval_id: unique integer id   - article_id: integer article ID   - version_id: integer article version ID   - approver: username string   - status: enumerated string ["approved", "rejected", "revision_requested"]   - comments: free text comments string   - timestamp: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - Example rows:   ``` 1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00 2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00 3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00 ```  ### 5. workflow_stages.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required` - Meaning:   - stage_id: unique integer stage identifier   - category: article category string   - stage_name: name of workflow stage string   - stage_order: integer order of workflow stages   - is_required: string "yes" or "no" indicating if stage is mandatory - Example rows:   ``` 1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes 2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes 3|news|Editor Review|1|yes 4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes 5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes ```  ### 6. comments.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp` - Meaning:   - comment_id: unique integer id   - article_id: integer article ID   - version_id: integer article version ID   - user: username string   - comment_text: comment content string   - timestamp: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - Example rows:   ``` 1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00 2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00 3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00 ```  ### 7. analytics.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares` - Meaning:   - analytics_id: unique integer id   - article_id: integer   - date: YYYY-MM-DD   - views: integer total page views   - unique_visitors: integer unique visitors   - avg_time_seconds: integer average duration of visit in seconds   - shares: integer social shares count - Example rows:   ``` 1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12 2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18 3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15 4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8 ```  ---  This design specification document fully aligns with user requirements to enable backend and frontend development concurrently and independently.

# ContentPublishingHub Web Application Specification

## Objective
Build a comprehensive content management system with version control, content scheduling, and analytics. The system supports collaborative content creation with version history tracking, editorial comments, and content analytics. Testing should start from the Dashboard page.

## Language
Python (Flask framework recommended)

## Page Design

### 1. Dashboard Page (`dashboard.html`)
**Route:** `/dashboard`
**Purpose:** Main landing page showing content overview and navigation
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `dashboard-page`)
- Welcome message with username (id: `welcome-message`)
- Quick stats section (id: `quick-stats`)
- Create Article button (id: `create-article-button`)
- Recent activity feed (id: `recent-activity`)

### 2. Create Article Page (`create_article.html`)
**Route:** `/article/create`
**Purpose:** Editor for creating new articles
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `create-article-page`)
- Article title input (id: `article-title`)
- Content editor textarea (id: `article-content`)
- Save as Draft button (id: `save-draft-button`)
- Cancel button (id: `cancel-button`)

### 3. Edit Article Page (`edit_article.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit`
**Purpose:** Edit existing article with version tracking
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `edit-article-page`)
- Article title input (id: `edit-article-title`)
- Content editor textarea (id: `edit-article-content`)
- Save New Version button (id: `save-version-button`)
- Cancel button (id: `cancel-edit`)

### 4. Article Version History Page (`version_history.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions`
**Purpose:** View all versions and restore previous versions
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `version-history-page`)
- Versions list (id: `versions-list`)
- Version comparison section (id: `version-comparison`)
- Restore button (id: `restore-version-1`)
- Back to Edit button (id: `back-to-edit-history`)

### 5. My Articles Page (`my_articles.html`)
**Route:** `/articles/mine`
**Purpose:** List user's articles with filters
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `my-articles-page`)
- Filter by status dropdown (id: `filter-article-status`)
- Articles table (id: `articles-table`)
- Create New Article button (id: `create-new-article`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard`)

### 6. Published Articles Page (`published_articles.html`)
**Route:** `/articles/published`
**Purpose:** Public-facing content library
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `published-articles-page`)
- Filter by category dropdown (id: `filter-published-category`)
- Articles grid (id: `published-articles-grid`)
- Sort by dropdown (id: `sort-published`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-published`)

### 7. Content Calendar Page (`content_calendar.html`)
**Route:** `/calendar`
**Purpose:** Scheduled publications timeline view
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `calendar-page`)
- Calendar view selector (id: `calendar-view`)
- Calendar grid (id: `calendar-grid`)
- Schedule button (id: `schedule-button`)
- Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`)

### 8. Article Analytics Page (`article_analytics.html`)
**Route:** `/article/<article_id>/analytics`
**Purpose:** View engagement metrics for published article
**Elements:**
- Page container (id: `analytics-page`)
- Analytics overview (id: `analytics-overview`)
- Total views (id: `analytics-total-views`)
- Unique visitors (id: `analytics-unique-visitors`)
- Back to Article button (id: `back-to-article-analytics`)

## Data Storage

The 'ContentPublishingHub' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. users.txt
Format: `username|email|fullname|created_date`
Example:
```
john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15
alice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16
bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17
admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10
```

### 2. articles.txt
Format: `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date`
Example:
```
1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00
2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00
3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24|
```
status: draft, pending_review, under_review, approved, published, rejected, archived
category: news, blog, tutorial, announcement, press_release

### 3. article_versions.txt
Format: `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary`
Example:
```
1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft
2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction
3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version
```

### 4. approvals.txt
Format: `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp`
Example:
```
1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00
2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00
3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00
```
status: approved, rejected, revision_requested

### 5. workflow_stages.txt
Format: `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required`
Example:
```
1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes
2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes
3|news|Editor Review|1|yes
4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes
5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes
```

### 6. comments.txt
Format: `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp`
Example:
```
1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00
2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00
3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00
```

### 7. analytics.txt
Format: `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares`
Example:
```
1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12
2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18
3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15
4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8
```


=== design_spec.md ===
# ContentPublishingHub Design Specification

## Flask Routes Specification

### 1. Dashboard Page
- Route URL: `/dashboard`
- HTTP Methods: GET
- Function Handler Name: `dashboard`
- Template Rendered: `dashboard.html`
- Context Variables to Template:
  - `username` (str): current logged-in user's name
  - `quick_stats` (dict): statistics summary
  - `recent_activity` (list): recent user activity entries

### 2. Create Article Page
- Route URL: `/article/create`
- HTTP Methods: GET, POST
- Function Handler Name: `create_article`
- Template Rendered: `create_article.html`
- Context Variables:
  - For GET: none or blank form
  - For POST: validation errors, success messages

### 3. Edit Article Page
- Route URL: `/article/<article_id>/edit`
- HTTP Methods: GET, POST
- Function Handler Name: `edit_article`
- Template Rendered: `edit_article.html`
- Context Variables:
  - `article` (dict): article details including current title and content

### 4. Article Version History Page
- Route URL: `/article/<article_id>/versions`
- HTTP Methods: GET
- Function Handler Name: `version_history`
- Template Rendered: `version_history.html`
- Context Variables:
  - `versions` (list): list of versions with metadata for the article
  - `current_version_id` (int)

### 5. My Articles Page
- Route URL: `/articles/mine`
- HTTP Methods: GET
- Function Handler Name: `my_articles`
- Template Rendered: `my_articles.html`
- Context Variables:
  - `articles` (list): user's articles filtered by status
  - `filter_status` (str): current filter status

### 6. Published Articles Page
- Route URL: `/articles/published`
- HTTP Methods: GET
- Function Handler Name: `published_articles`
- Template Rendered: `published_articles.html`
- Context Variables:
  - `articles` (list): published articles filtered by category and sort
  - `filter_category` (str)
  - `sort_order` (str)

### 7. Content Calendar Page
- Route URL: `/calendar`
- HTTP Methods: GET
- Function Handler Name: `content_calendar`
- Template Rendered: `content_calendar.html`
- Context Variables:
  - `calendar_view` (str): current selected calendar view
  - `scheduled_articles` (list): scheduled articles for display

### 8. Article Analytics Page
- Route URL: `/article/<article_id>/analytics`
- HTTP Methods: GET
- Function Handler Name: `article_analytics`
- Template Rendered: `article_analytics.html`
- Context Variables:
  - `analytics_data` (dict): engagement metrics for article


## HTML Templates Specification

### `dashboard.html`
- Page container id: `dashboard-page`
- Elements:
  - Welcome message with id `welcome-message`
  - Quick stats section id `quick-stats`
  - Create Article button id `create-article-button` (link to `/article/create`)
  - Recent activity feed id `recent-activity`

### `create_article.html`
- Page container id: `create-article-page`
- Form elements:
  - Article title input id `article-title`
  - Content editor textarea id `article-content`
  - Save as Draft button id `save-draft-button`
  - Cancel button id `cancel-button` (navigates back or dashboard)

### `edit_article.html`
- Page container id: `edit-article-page`
- Form elements:
  - Article title input id `edit-article-title`
  - Content editor textarea id `edit-article-content`
  - Save New Version button id `save-version-button`
  - Cancel button id `cancel-edit`

### `version_history.html`
- Page container id: `version-history-page`
- Versions list container id: `versions-list` (listing all versions)
- Version comparison section id: `version-comparison`
- Restore button specifically: `restore-version-1` for version 1 and similar pattern for others
- Back to Edit button id: `back-to-edit-history`

### `my_articles.html`
- Page container id: `my-articles-page`
- Filter dropdown id: `filter-article-status`
- Articles table id: `articles-table`
- Create New Article button id: `create-new-article` (links to `/article/create`)
- Back to Dashboard button id: `back-to-dashboard`

### `published_articles.html`
- Page container id: `published-articles-page`
- Filter by category dropdown id: `filter-published-category`
- Articles grid container id: `published-articles-grid`
- Sort by dropdown id: `sort-published`
- Back to Dashboard button id: `back-to-dashboard-published`

### `content_calendar.html`
- Page container id: `calendar-page`
- Calendar view selector dropdown id: `calendar-view`
- Calendar grid id: `calendar-grid`
- Schedule button id: `schedule-button`
- Back to Dashboard button id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`

### `article_analytics.html`
- Page container id: `analytics-page`
- Analytics overview section id: `analytics-overview`
- Total views display id: `analytics-total-views`
- Unique visitors display id: `analytics-unique-visitors`
- Back to Article button id: `back-to-article-analytics`


## Data File Schemas Specification

### 1. users.txt
- Pipe-delimited fields:
  `username|email|fullname|created_date`
- Meaning:
  - username: unique user identifier (string)
  - email: user email address (string)
  - fullname: full name of user (string)
  - created_date: account creation date (YYYY-MM-DD string)
- Example rows:
  ```
john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15
alice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16
bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17
admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10
```

### 2. articles.txt
- Pipe-delimited fields:
  `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date`
- Meaning:
  - article_id: integer unique article ID
  - title: article title string
  - author: username string
  - category: enumerated string values ["news", "blog", "tutorial", "announcement", "press_release"]
  - status: enumerated string values ["draft", "pending_review", "under_review", "approved", "published", "rejected", "archived"]
  - tags: comma-separated string list
  - featured_image: URL path string or empty
  - meta_description: short description string
  - created_date: date string YYYY-MM-DD
  - publish_date: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS or empty if unpublished
- Example rows:
  ```
1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00
2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00
3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24|
```

### 3. article_versions.txt
- Pipe-delimited fields:
  `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary`
- Meaning:
  - version_id: unique integer version id
  - article_id: integer article ID
  - version_number: integer (incrementing version number)
  - content: string with full article text
  - author: username string
  - created_date: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
  - change_summary: brief string description of changes
- Example rows:
  ```
1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft
2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction
3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version
```

### 4. approvals.txt
- Pipe-delimited fields:
  `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp`
- Meaning:
  - approval_id: unique integer id
  - article_id: integer article ID
  - version_id: integer article version ID
  - approver: username string
  - status: enumerated string ["approved", "rejected", "revision_requested"]
  - comments: free text comments string
  - timestamp: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
- Example rows:
  ```
1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00
2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00
3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00
```

### 5. workflow_stages.txt
- Pipe-delimited fields:
  `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required`
- Meaning:
  - stage_id: unique integer stage identifier
  - category: article category string
  - stage_name: name of workflow stage string
  - stage_order: integer order of workflow stages
  - is_required: string "yes" or "no" indicating if stage is mandatory
- Example rows:
  ```
1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes
2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes
3|news|Editor Review|1|yes
4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes
5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes
```

### 6. comments.txt
- Pipe-delimited fields:
  `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp`
- Meaning:
  - comment_id: unique integer id
  - article_id: integer article ID
  - version_id: integer article version ID
  - user: username string
  - comment_text: comment content string
  - timestamp: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
- Example rows:
  ```
1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00
2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00
3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00
```

### 7. analytics.txt
- Pipe-delimited fields:
  `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares`
- Meaning:
  - analytics_id: unique integer id
  - article_id: integer
  - date: YYYY-MM-DD
  - views: integer total page views
  - unique_visitors: integer unique visitors
  - avg_time_seconds: integer average duration of visit in seconds
  - shares: integer social shares count
- Example rows:
  ```
1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12
2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18
3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15
4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8
```

---

This design specification document fully aligns with user requirements to enable backend and frontend development concurrently and independently.

  [user_task_description] # ContentPublishingHub Web Application Specification  ## Objective Build a comprehensive content management system with version control, content scheduling, and analytics. The system supports collaborative content creation with version history tracking, editorial comments, and content analytics. Testing should start from the Dashboard page.  ## Language Python (Flask framework recommended)  ## Page Design  ### 1. Dashboard Page (`dashboard.html`) **Route:** `/dashboard` **Purpose:** Main landing page showing content overview and navigation **Elements:** - Page container (id: `dashboard-page`) - Welcome message with username (id: `welcome-message`) - Quick stats section (id: `quick-stats`) - Create Article button (id: `create-article-button`) - Recent activity feed (id: `recent-activity`)  ### 2. Create Article Page (`create_article.html`) **Route:** `/article/create` **Purpose:** Editor for creating new articles **Elements:** - Page container (id: `create-article-page`) - Article title input (id: `article-title`) - Content editor textarea (id: `article-content`) - Save as Draft button (id: `save-draft-button`) - Cancel button (id: `cancel-button`)  ### 3. Edit Article Page (`edit_article.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/edit` **Purpose:** Edit existing article with version tracking **Elements:** - Page container (id: `edit-article-page`) - Article title input (id: `edit-article-title`) - Content editor textarea (id: `edit-article-content`) - Save New Version button (id: `save-version-button`) - Cancel button (id: `cancel-edit`)  ### 4. Article Version History Page (`version_history.html`) **Route:** `/article/<article_id>/versions` **Purpose:** View all versions and restore previous versions **Elements:** - Page container (id: `version-history-page`) - Versions list (id: `versions-list`) - Version comparison section (id: `version-comparison`) - Restore button (id: `restore-version-1`) - Back to Edit button (id: `back-to-edit-history`)  ### 5. My Articles Page (`my_articles.html`) **Route:** `/articles/mine` **Purpose:** List user's articles with filters **Elements:** - Page container (id: `my-articles-page`) - Filter by status dropdown (id: `filter-article-status`) - Articles table (id: `articles-table`) - Create New Article button (id: `create-new-article`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard`)  ### 6. Published Articles Page (`published_articles.html`) **Route:** `/articles/published` **Purpose:** Public-facing content library **Elements:** - Page container (id: `published-articles-page`) - Filter by category dropdown (id: `filter-published-category`) - Articles grid (id: `published-articles-grid`) - Sort by dropdown (id: `sort-published`) - Back to Dashboard button (id: `back-to-dashboard-published`)  ### 7. results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00 3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24| ``` status: draft, pending_review, under_review, approved, published, rejected, archived category: news, blog, tutorial, announcement, press_release  ### 3. article_versions.txt Format: `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary` Example: ``` 1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft 2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction 3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version ```  ### 4. approvals.txt Format: 5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes ```  ### 6. comments.txt Format: `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp` Example: ``` 1|1|1|alice|Great start! Page container id: `published-articles-page` - Filter by category dropdown id: `filter-published-category` - Articles grid container id: `published-articles-grid` - Sort by dropdown id: `sort-published` - Back to Dashboard button id: `back-to-dashboard-published`  ### `content_calendar.html` - Page container id: `calendar-page` - Calendar view selector dropdown id: `calendar-view` - Calendar grid id: `calendar-grid` - Schedule button id: `schedule-button` - Back to Dashboard button id: `back-to-dashboard-calendar`  ### `article_analytics.html` - Page container id: `analytics-page` - Analytics overview section id: `analytics-overview` - Total views display id: `analytics-total-views` - Unique visitors display id: `analytics-unique-visitors` - Back to Article button id: `back-to-article-analytics`   ## Data File Schemas Specification  ### 1. users.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `username|email|fullname|created_date` - Meaning:   - username: unique user identifier (string)   - email: user email address (string)   - fullname: full name of user (string)   - created_date: account creation date (YYYY-MM-DD string) - Example rows:   ``` john|john@example.com|John Doe|2024-01-15 alice|alice@example.com|Alice Smith|2024-01-16 bob|bob@example.com|Bob Johnson|2024-01-17 admin|admin@example.com|Admin User|2024-01-10 ```  ### 2. articles.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `article_id|title|author|category|status|tags|featured_image|meta_description|created_date|publish_date` - Meaning:   - article_id: integer unique article ID   - title: article title string   - author: username string   - category: enumerated string values ["news", "blog", "tutorial", "announcement", "press_release"]   - status: enumerated string values ["draft", "pending_review", "under_review", "approved", "published", "rejected", "archived"]   - tags: comma-separated string list   - featured_image: URL path string or empty   - meta_description: short description string   - created_date: date string YYYY-MM-DD   - publish_date: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS or empty if unpublished - Example rows:   ``` 1|Introduction to Flask|john|tutorial|published|python,flask,web|/img/flask.jpg|Learn Flask basics|2024-01-20|2024-01-22 10:00:00 2|Company Quarterly Update|alice|announcement|approved|company,news||Q1 results|2024-01-23|2024-01-25 09:00:00 3|New Product Launch|john|press_release|draft|product,launch|/img/product.jpg|Exciting new release|2024-01-24| ```  ### 3. article_versions.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|created_date|change_summary` - Meaning:   - version_id: unique integer version id   - article_id: integer article ID   - version_number: integer (incrementing version number)   - content: string with full article text   - author: username string   - created_date: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS   - change_summary: brief string description of changes - Example rows:   ``` 1|1|1|Flask is a micro web framework...|john|2024-01-20 14:30:00|Initial draft 2|1|2|Flask is a lightweight web framework...|john|2024-01-21 09:15:00|Improved introduction 3|2|1|We are pleased to announce...|alice|2024-01-23 11:00:00|Initial version ```  ### 4. approvals.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp` - Meaning:   - approval_id: unique integer id   - article_id: integer article ID   - version_id: integer article version ID   - approver: username string   - status: enumerated string ["approved", "rejected", "revision_requested"]   - comments: free text comments string   - timestamp: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - Example rows:   ``` 1|1|2|alice|approved|Looks good, well written|2024-01-21 10:30:00 2|1|2|bob|approved|Ready for publication|2024-01-21 15:00:00 3|3|1|alice|rejected|Needs more details in section 2|2024-01-24 14:00:00 ```  ### 5. workflow_stages.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `stage_id|category|stage_name|stage_order|is_required` - Meaning:   - stage_id: unique integer stage identifier   - category: article category string   - stage_name: name of workflow stage string   - stage_order: integer order of workflow stages   - is_required: string "yes" or "no" indicating if stage is mandatory - Example rows:   ``` 1|tutorial|Editor Review|1|yes 2|tutorial|Publisher Approval|2|yes 3|news|Editor Review|1|yes 4|announcement|Editor Review|1|yes 5|announcement|Publisher Approval|2|yes ```  ### 6. comments.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `comment_id|article_id|version_id|user|comment_text|timestamp` - Meaning:   - comment_id: unique integer id   - article_id: integer article ID   - version_id: integer article version ID   - user: username string   - comment_text: comment content string   - timestamp: datetime string YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - Example rows:   ``` 1|1|1|alice|Great start! Consider adding more examples.|2024-01-20 16:00:00 2|1|2|bob|The flow is much better now.|2024-01-21 11:00:00 3|3|1|alice|Section 2 needs expansion - add metrics.|2024-01-24 13:45:00 ```  ### 7. analytics.txt - Pipe-delimited fields:   `analytics_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares` - Meaning:   - analytics_id: unique integer id   - article_id: integer   - date: YYYY-MM-DD   - views: integer total page views   - unique_visitors: integer unique visitors   - avg_time_seconds: integer average duration of visit in seconds   - shares: integer social shares count - Example rows:   ``` 1|1|2024-01-22|150|120|245|12 2|1|2024-01-23|230|180|210|18 3|1|2024-01-24|180|140|220|15 4|2|2024-01-25|95|75|180|8 ```  ---  This design specification document fully aligns with user requirements to enable backend and frontend development concurrently and independently.

[Tool_Request] SystemArchitect: write_text_file({"filename":"architecture.md","content":"# ContentPublishingHub Backend Architecture Design Document\n\n---\n\n## Section 1: Backend System Design\n\n### 1. Flask Application Structure\n\nThe Flask application is structured into modular packages and submodules to separate concerns, enable scalability, and facilitate testing and maintenance. The proposed structure is:\n\n```\n/content_publishing_hub\n|-- app.py                # Flask app factory and initialization\n|-- config.py             # Configurations (env, file paths, secret keys, etc.)\n|-- /routes               # Routing and controller functions\n|   |-- __init__.py\n|   |-- dashboard.py\n|   |-- article.py\n|   |-- analytics.py\n|   |-- calendar.py\n|-- /services             # Business logic and data manipulation\n|   |-- __init__.py\n|   |-- article_service.py\n|   |-- user_service.py\n|   |-- version_control_service.py\n|   |-- analytics_service.py\n|-- /data_access          # Data access layer - file I/O abstraction\n|   |-- __init__.py\n|   |-- articles_dao.py\n|   |-- users_dao.py\n|   |-- versions_dao.py\n|   |-- approvals_dao.py\n|   |-- comments_dao.py\n|   |-- analytics_dao.py\n|-- /utils                # Utility functions\n|   |-- __init__.py\n|   |-- file_io.py        # Read/write helper functions using pipe-delimited format\n|   |-- validators.py\n|-- /templates            # Jinja2 templates for frontend\n|-- /static               # Static assets\n|-- requirements.txt\n|-- README.md\n```\n\n### 2. Module Responsibilities\n\n- **app.py**: Application factory to create the Flask app instance, register Blueprints (routes), and initialize middleware if needed (e.g., session handling, authentication, error handlers).\n\n- **routes/**: Contains Blueprints for grouping related routes and controller functions by feature (e.g., dashboard, article management, analytics).\n  - Handles HTTP requests and responses, input validation at the endpoint level,\n  - Parses URL parameters and form data,\n  - Calls appropriate services,\n  - Passes context data to template rendering.\n\n- **services/**: Implements core business logic and workflows.\n  - Article management including create/edit, versioning coordination, approval workflow logic.\n  - User-related operations.\n  - Analytics aggregation and computation.\n\n- **data_access/**: Abstracts file reading and writing operations.\n  - Provides functions to fetch, parse, update, and save data in the pipe-delimited text file formats.\n  - Ensures thread-safe read/write to data files to prevent data corruption.\n\n- **utils/**:\n  - Common helper functions for file I/O, pipe-delimited parsing and serialization,\n  - Input validation.\n\n### 3. Data File Access and Management\n\nAll persistent data is stored in pipe-delimited text files under a data directory (`data/*.txt`). Data access layer functions:\n\n- Load data into application objects or dictionaries on demand (lazy loading for performance).\n- Support CRUD operations conceptually — reading full datasets to modify and write back, or appending for new records.\n- Each DAO module corresponds to one or more data files, parsing them into relevant data structures.\n- Version controlled files like `article_versions.txt`, `approvals.txt`, and `comments.txt` are accessed in a way to preserve integrity.\n- Timestamp and ID generation managed via services or utility modules to avoid collisions.\n\n### 4. Data Flow Between Components\n\n1. User sends HTTP request to Flask endpoint in routes module.\n2. Route function extracts input (URL params, form fields), validates data.\n3. Route invokes service function passing inputs.\n4. Service uses DAOs to read or update data files as needed.\n5. Service returns processed data or status to route.\n6. Route renders appropriate Jinja2 template, passing context data.\n7. Client receives rendered HTML page.\n\nThis layered approach enforces separation of concerns and easy unit testing.\n\n---\n\n## Section 2: Routing and API Endpoints\n\n| Route URL                                | HTTP Methods | Function Handler    | Input                               | Output                                   | Notes/Details                                      |\n|------------------------------------------|--------------|--------------------|-----------------------------------|------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|\n| `/dashboard`                             | GET          | `dashboard`         | Session user info (username)      | Render `dashboard.html` with: `username`, `quick_stats`, `recent_activity` |\n| `/article/create`                        | GET, POST    | `create_article`    | GET: none (empty form)             | GET: blank form rendered                       |\n|                                          |              |                    | POST: form data (title, content) | POST: validation errors or success messages     |\n| `/article/<int:article_id>/edit`         | GET, POST    | `edit_article`       | GET: article_id URL param          | `edit_article.html` with `article` dict            |\n|                                          |              |                    | POST: update form data             | validation errors or success messages             |\n| `/article/<int:article_id>/versions`     | GET          | `version_history`    | article_id URL param               | `version_history.html` with list of versions and current version id |\n| `/articles/mine`                         | GET          | `my_articles`       | Session user info, optional filter parameter (status) | `my_articles.html` with filtered articles list and filter status |\n| `/articles/published`                    | GET          | `published_articles` | Optional category and sort filter query params  | `published_articles.html` with filtered and sorted articles |\n| `/calendar`                             | GET          | `content_calendar`  | Optional calendar view selection  | `content_calendar.html` with calendar view and scheduled articles |\n| `/article/<int:article_id>/analytics`   | GET          | `article_analytics`  | article_id URL param               | `article_analytics.html` with `analytics_data` dict |\n\n### Dynamic Routes Pattern\n- Use `<int:article_id>` for articles.\n- Article version specific routes embed `<article_id>` but could be extended for `<version_id>` if needed.\n\n### Session and Authentication\n- Current user session stored via Flask session or equivalent, including `username`.\n- User authentication middleware integrated but not detailed here.\n- Endpoints assume session-based access; future token-based API extensions possible.\n\n---\n\n## Section 3: Version Control Design\n\n### Versioning Workflow Architecture\n\n- Each article can have multiple versions tracked in `article_versions.txt`.\n- Version entry includes version number, content, author, timestamp, and change summary.\n- Version numbers auto-increment per article.\n\n### Approval Workflow\n\n- Approvals tied to particular article versions stored in `approvals.txt`.\n- Each approval includes approver username, status (`approved`, `rejected`, `revision_requested`), comment, and timestamp.\n- Workflow stages from `workflow_stages.txt` provide sequence and requirement flags per article category.\n- Business logic enforces that an article version must pass all required stages before final publication.\n\n### Comments on Versions\n\n- Comments linked to article version via `comments.txt`.\n- Multiple users can leave comments, facilitating review discussions.\n\n### Storage Interaction\n\n- Services read/write the version-related files via DAOs ensuring atomic updates.\n- New version creation triggers appending to `article_versions.txt`.\n- Approval status updates and comments are appended to their respective files.\n- Version history queries aggregate versions, approvals, and comments to display coherent version metadata.\n\n---\n\n## Section 4: Frontend Integration Points\n\n### Data Passing to Templates\n\n- Context variables listed in design_spec.md for each route are populated by backend via `render_template` calls.\n- Backend ensures correct data types and structures:\n  - Primitive types (strings, ints) are passed directly.\n  - Lists and dicts are passed as JSON-friendly structures for ease of client rendering.\n\n### Endpoint Responses\n\n- All routes render full HTML templates as described, not JSON APIs, supporting server-side rendering.\n- For POST requests (e.g., create/edit article), response includes validation feedback within the same template.\n\n### Coordination Points with Frontend\n\n- Element IDs in templates (e.g., `create-article-button`, `versions-list`) are expected by frontend JS and styling.\n- Data structures such as list of articles, versions, analytics data must align with frontend expectations (e.g., keys, nested fields).\n- Pagination or filtering parameters passed as query parameters need consistent handling.\n\n---\n\nThis design document provides a comprehensive blueprint for backend and frontend teams to implement the ContentPublishingHub application effectively and independently.\n\n---\n\n_End of document_"})
